title: Add icon-only trigger mode to text-reader with floating toolbar
description:
## Context

text-reader currently renders a full toolbar inline (play/pause/stop buttons, voice selector, speed controls). When used in page-tools or article toolbars, it needs a compact icon-only mode — a single speaker icon that opens the full controls as a floating toolbar.

## Current State
- text-reader renders its full UI inline on setup()
- No variant/mode attribute for compact rendering
- The toolbar includes: play, pause, stop, voice select, speed controls
- Uses SpeechSynthesis API with word highlighting

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Add icon-only trigger mode
- New attribute: `variant='icon'` (or detect when inside page-tools)
- Renders a single speaker/volume icon button
- Clicking the icon starts reading AND shows a floating mini-toolbar
- The floating toolbar has: play/pause, stop, speed up/down
- Voice selection stays in the expanded view only

### 2. Floating mini-toolbar
- Appears above or below the icon button (like selection-menu positioning)
- Compact horizontal layout: [play/pause] [stop] [speed -] [speed +]
- Uses popover API for show/hide
- Auto-dismisses when reading stops
- Theme-aware styling matching selection-menu

### 3. Update demo
- article-reader-tools.html: text-reader in the article toolbar shows as speaker icon
- Clicking it starts reading with floating controls

### 4. Documentation
- Update text-reader doc page with icon variant
- Update text-reader demos
- Show integration with page-tools

### Files
- src/web-components/text-reader/logic.js — add icon mode + floating toolbar
- src/web-components/text-reader/styles.css — floating toolbar styles
- demos/examples/demos/article-reader-tools.html — update demo
- site/src/pages/docs/elements/web-components/text-reader.html — update docs
close_reason: Implemented variant=icon mode with floating mini-toolbar, updated demo and docs

title: Create subgrid demos to evaluate complexity reduction
description:
Create before/after demos comparing traditional approaches with CSS subgrid to evaluate whether it can reduce code complexity in Vanilla Breeze.

## Reference
See r-n-d/subgrid-exploration.md for full implementation details and CSS code.

## Demo Files to Create
1. docs/examples/demos/subgrid-cards.html - Card grid with aligned headers/footers
2. docs/examples/demos/subgrid-form.html - Form labels aligning across fieldsets
3. docs/examples/demos/subgrid-dl.html - Definition lists with consistent alignment
4. docs/examples/demos/subgrid-article.html - Article + aside sharing row grid

## Each Demo Should Include
- Side-by-side before (current) and after (subgrid) examples
- Code snippets showing CSS difference
- Visual debug grid lines showing alignment

## Evaluation Criteria
After creating demos, evaluate:
- Does subgrid meaningfully reduce CSS complexity?
- Are alignment benefits visible and valuable?
- Any edge cases where subgrid causes problems?

## Decision Point
Based on evaluation:
- If beneficial: Add [data-subgrid] utilities to layout-attributes.css
- If marginal: Document as optional technique only
- If problematic: Document findings, skip integration

## Browser Support
Subgrid is Baseline 2023 - Chrome 117+, Firefox 71+, Safari 16+

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 4 demo files created with before/after comparison
- [ ] Alignment benefits clearly visible in demos
- [ ] Written evaluation of complexity reduction
- [ ] Decision documented on whether to add to core

title: drag-surface: auto-apply cursor:grab to drag handles via CSS
description:
The drag-surface component requires users to manually add `cursor: grab` inline styles to drag handle elements. This should be handled automatically via CSS when a data-* attribute (e.g. data-drag-handle) is present on an element.

**Current state:**
- Demo and docs show inline `style="cursor: grab"` on drag handle elements
- Users must remember to add this styling manually

**Proposed changes:**
1. Check if a data attribute already identifies drag handles (e.g. data-drag-handle) — if not, add one
2. Add `cursor: grab` to `[data-drag-handle]` in styles.css (and `cursor: grabbing` on :active)
3. Update demos and docs to remove inline cursor styles
4. Verify logic.js uses the same attribute for handle detection

**Why:** Inline styles are an anti-pattern in VB. If the component already knows which elements are drag handles, it should style them automatically.
close_reason: Added cursor:grab/grabbing and user-select:none to [data-drag-handle] in component CSS. Removed inline styles from demo and docs.

title: page-tools toolbar robustness across extreme themes (sizing, clipping, stroke)
description:
User reports the page-tools toolbar (print / share / text-reader / watch) and the adjacent page-toc look broken in multiple extreme themes. Investigation shows three distinct root causes, but they cluster in the same region of the UI and should probably be fixed together or at least in the same sprint.

## Root cause A — theme `button` rules outspecificity the page-tools normalization (BIGGEST)

Several extreme themes wrap their button overrides like:

    :root[data-theme="editorial"] {
      & button,
      & .button {
        padding: 0.75rem 1.5rem;
        border: 1px solid var(--color-border-strong);
        ...
      }
    }

Selector specificity of that rule: `:root[data-theme="editorial"] button` = (0, 2, 1).

Page-tools' current normalization:

    page-tools watch-wc[data-variant="icon"] > button { all: unset; padding: var(--size-2xs); aspect-ratio: 1; ... }

Specificity = (0, 1, 3). **Theme wins (0,2,1) > (0,1,3) on the class-level column.** That's why Editorial shows buttons ~2.5× normal, Cyber wraps, 8-bit is chunky, NES / Win9x push icons down.

### Fix A

Bump the page-tools normalization selector's specificity so it beats any `:root[data-theme="…"] button` rule across all themes. Cleanest route: add an extra attribute that every docs-site page-tools already has. Either:

- `page-tools[data-orientation] print-page .print-trigger, …` → (0, 2, 3)
- or `:root page-tools …` → also (0, 2, 3) and doesn't require every caller to set data-orientation.

Apply to the base normalization AND the pressed-state rules (hover / focus / pressed). Verify across: editorial, 8bit, cyber, nes, win9x, swiss.

### Affected themes (by user screenshots)
- Editorial — buttons 2.5× size, wrap to 2×2
- Cyber — 2× size, wraps 3+1
- 8-bit — chunky
- NES, Win9x — size issue pushing icons down
- Swiss — selected icon reads smaller than siblings (probably theme-specific aria-pressed override; verify after Fix A lands)

## Root cause B — aside `overflow-y: auto` clips theme outset shadows/filters

Neumorphism and Claymorphism themes use multi-layer `box-shadow` for the emboss/squeeze effect. The sticky docs aside has `overflow-y: auto` (from layout-sidebar) which clips any paint outside the element's box — including shadows. Hence the corner clipping + small distortions at the page-toc and page-tools bounds in the user's Neumorphism and Clay screenshots.

### Fix B

Options, pick one:
1. Drop `overflow-y: auto` on the sticky aside and rely on page-level scroll instead. Changes the body/page scroll ownership — might regress b1v6's sticky coherence fix.
2. Keep `overflow` but add `padding: var(--size-s)` on the inside of the aside so the shadow has room to paint without being clipped.
3. Emit the theme's emboss via `filter: drop-shadow()` instead of `box-shadow` when the parent has overflow clipping — rework of theme rendering. Largest change.

Option 2 is the cheapest and least likely to regress anything else. Try first.

### Affected themes
- Neumorphism (bad)
- Claymorphism (small)

## Root cause C — theme-specific quirks

- **Rough** — right-side horizontal scroll appears. Probably a theme pseudo-element (SVG filter, hand-drawn border) overflows past the body edge. Investigate `_extreme-rough.css` for `right`/`transform` rules on scroll-affecting elements.
- **Super2026** — the filled pressed state loses the visual outline indicator. Likely the theme's own `aria-pressed`/`:focus-visible` override beats ours; or the interactive token resolves to the same colour as the surround. Lower priority — the state still reads as filled.

### Fix C

Each is a one-theme patch. Can be addressed after A and B.

## Verification

For each theme in the theme picker, load /docs/typography/ (long page, plenty of toolbar + toc) and confirm:
- Toolbar icons are all ~30×30 (or theme-appropriate small)
- Toolbar fits on one line inside the narrow aside
- page-toc and page-tools don't clip at their corners
- No horizontal body scroll
- The watch pressed state reads as "yes, committed" regardless of theme (filled, ideally with the bookmark-check)

## Out of scope

- Redesigning the page-tools variants
- Changing the sticky-aside pattern
- Brand-theme demo site specifics

## Recommended order

1. **Fix A first** — it's the biggest single lever and closes 5+ reported themes in one commit.
2. **Fix B** — once A lands, verify Neumorphism/Clay corner clipping is still present, then ship the padding fix.
3. **Fix C** — Rough and Super2026 get one-theme patches if still visible after A + B.

comments.author: tpowell
comments.text:
Progress 2026-04-23:

✅ Fix A shipped (9d631511): `:root` specificity bump on page-tools
   normalization beats theme `:root[data-theme="..."] button` rules.
   Editorial, 8-bit, Cyber, NES, Win9x, Swiss all measure 30×30 now.

✅ Fix B shipped (39f6d105): `padding-inline: var(--size-2xs)` on the
   sticky aside gives neumorphism/clay room to paint outset shadows
   without the overflow:auto clipping soft corners.

🔸 Fix C (open): Rough theme overflows the body by ~121px. Culprit is
   the settings-panel popover (`.panel` 256px wide) being treated as a
   `.card` by Rough's override, which adds padding: 2rem — pushing its
   right-aligned popover past the viewport. Body overflow-x: hidden
   suppresses actual scroll, but the scrollWidth > viewportWidth shows
   up as a scrollbar indicator in some browsers.

   Fix options:
   a) Scope Rough's `.card` padding to not apply to `.panel`/popovers
   b) Add `max-inline-size: min(content, 100vw - 2rem)` to settings-panel
   c) Reduce Rough's card padding from 2rem to 1.5rem globally

   Recommend (a): scope the theme's `.card` rule to `main .card` only.

🔸 Super2026's "no outline on pressed state" is expected (border-color:
   transparent on filled watch, per fafc7d9f). Not a bug. Closing as
   observation only.

title: Create <mobile-menu> custom element for responsive navigation
description:
## Context The doc site's mobile menu is implemented as raw HTML + CSS in demos/docs.css with no framework component. The hamburger button, popover panel, and responsive show/hide are tightly coupled across generate-sidebars.js and docs.css. This makes it hard for themes to customize and for developers to reuse. ## Current State - Mobile menu is `
` — already semantic nav - Both header.html and mobile-menu.html are generated by `site/plugins/generate-sidebars.js` - Two separate mobile systems: header hamburger (popover) + sidebar drawer (checkbox hack) - All styling in `demos/docs.css` lines 532-780 — nothing in the framework - Single breakpoint at 52rem controls header nav hiding, mobile toggle, sidebar drawer, and main padding - Pattern docs recommend `
`-based mobile nav but site uses popover API ## Proposed Architecture Create `` as a CSS-first custom element (like `site-tools`, `brand-mark`): 1. **Encapsulate**: toggle button + popover panel + responsive breakpoint behavior 2. **Theme hooks**: CSS custom properties for colors, radius, shadows, backdrop 3. **Content model**: accepts slotted nav content (links, sections, search, theme picker) 4. **Breakpoint**: configurable via `data-breakpoint` attribute (default 52rem) 5. **Trigger**: built-in hamburger icon-wc, customizable via attribute ### Markup target: ```html
Vanilla Breeze

...
``` ### Key files to modify: - `src/custom-elements/mobile-menu/styles.css` — new - `src/custom-elements/mobile-menu/logic.js` — new (popover + breakpoint observer) - `src/custom-elements/mobile-menu/api.json` — new - `src/custom-elements/index.css` — add import - `site/plugins/generate-sidebars.js` — update generator - `demos/docs.css` — extract mobile menu styles into the component - Consider unifying with sidebar drawer system ### Considerations: - The responsive breakpoint currently controls both header nav hiding AND sidebar drawer — needs decoupling - The popover API approach is correct and modern — keep it - Progressive enhancement: works without JS via popover, JS adds breakpoint observer - Themes should be able to style via `--mobile-menu-*` tokens

title: Form bot protection: layered honeypot + timing + behavioural scoring + Turnstile escalation
description:
Implement the layered bot defence in `admin/r-n-d/FORM-BOT-STRATEGY.md`. VB ships forms today (`m4up` contact form, `o2vt` newsletter); none of them have bot protection yet, so every newly-deployed form is a free spam vector. This bead closes that gap before the production /go/email + /go/newsletter endpoints attract serious traffic. ## Strategy in one sentence Score every submission across HTML / JS / server signals; silently 200 the high-confidence bot ones (so they don't adapt), challenge the borderline ones with invisible Turnstile, flag the suspicious ones for review, accept the clean ones. No user-visible CAPTCHA on the happy path. ## Deliverables (5 layers, ship in order) **Layer 0 — AI agent friendliness (DO FIRST so we don't block legitimate automation)** - Schema.org `ContactPoint` JSON-LD on contact pages — depends on bead `28id` (JSON-LD support) - `data-bot-hint="AI agents: email …"` attribute support on `
` - `noindex, nofollow` meta on `/thank-you` pages **Layer 1 — Honeypot field (HTML, no JS dep)** - `
` baked into form patterns - `.form-trap` CSS class added to `src/utils/forms.css` (sr-only-style clip, NOT inline `display:none`) - Server check: any non-empty honeypot value → silentAccept() **Layer 2 — Signed timing token** - `` rendered at request time (Pages Function HTML transform OR a per-page meta helper for SSG) - HMAC verify on submit; score by token age (<1s = +80, <3s = +50, missing = +90) - Per-build static token ALSO acceptable for low-value contact forms (acknowledged limitation in spec) **Layer 3 — Behavioural signals (JS enhancement)** - New `enhanceBotProtection(form)` in `src/lib/form-field-enhancements.js` (or a sibling module). Activates when form has `data-bot-protection`. - Listens passively for: focus events, keydown on text fields, mousemove (skipped on touch-primary), submit time - Computes score per spec table; signs it; injects as hidden `_vb_score` field - Hooks into `vb:beforesubmit` (NEW — needs to be added to enhanceForm() per spec Open Question #2) **Layer 4 — Server-side verification (Cloudflare Worker / Pages Function)** - New `functions/_lib/bot-score.js` shared lib — verifyToken(), verifyClientScore(), rate-limit by CF-Connecting-IP via existing VB_KV - Wire into `/go/email` (functions/go/email.js) and `/go/newsletter/subscribe.js` so they call the scorer before doing real work - Escalation thresholds per spec: ≥80 silentAccept, 60–79 Turnstile, 30–59 acceptWithFlag, <30 process **Layer 5 — Turnstile peer dependency (optional, lazy-loaded)** - Loaded only when score lands in 60–79 AND `data-turnstile-sitekey` is set - Falls back to acceptWithFlag if no sitekey — Turnstile is genuinely optional ## Spec source `admin/r-n-d/FORM-BOT-STRATEGY.md` — 496 lines. Includes the full signal scoring table, escalation tree, complete data-* attribute contract, and reference JS for layers 3 + 4. ## Acceptance - A contact form with `data-bot-protection` rejects (200-fake-success) a curl POST that fills the honeypot. - A submit within 1s of page load scores ≥80 and is silently accepted. - A submit with no field-focus events scores ≥40 and is flagged. - Legitimate human submission (typed, mouse moved, ~10s on form) scores <30 and processes normally. - Turnstile widget is NOT loaded on the happy path — verify via DevTools Network panel. - AI agent that reads the page's JSON-LD finds the contact email and uses mailto: instead of POSTing — verified by inspecting the rendered head. - `/go/email` and `/go/newsletter/subscribe` both consult the scorer before processing. ## Out of scope - Full FORM-ENDPOINT-CONTRACT.md (separate spec, separate bead) — this bead focuses on the bot gate that runs BEFORE endpoint validation. - CSRF — distinct from `_vb_token` (which is a timing signal, not a CSRF token). Endpoint-contract bead covers it. - Account abuse / login bot defence — different threat model, different layers (probably needs Turnstile by default). - Disposable email domain list — optional Layer 4 signal noted in spec; ship without it for v1. - Settings UI for thresholds — hardcoded defaults are fine for v1. ## Phasing If this is too big for one bead, split as: - **Phase A**: Layer 0 + Layer 1 (AI-agent friendliness + honeypot) — small, immediate value - **Phase B**: Layers 2 + 4 (timing token + server scoring) — needs `vb:beforesubmit` event in enhanceForm() - **Phase C**: Layer 3 (JS behavioural signals) - **Phase D**: Layer 5 (Turnstile peer dep + sitekey wiring) Recommend doing A immediately as a separate sub-bead since it's literally a JSON-LD block + a meta tag + a doc note — no JS, no server, no risk. ## Closing bd close vanilla-breeze- --reason="Shipped 5-layer bot defence per FORM-BOT-STRATEGY.md. Contact + newsletter forms now silentAccept honeypot fills and sub-1s submissions, score borderline cases via signed timing token + behavioural signals, and escalate to invisible Turnstile only at 60–79. AI agents see Schema.org contact info and skip the form."

title: Glassmorphism theme: sticky header transparency shows content scrolling behind
description: The Glassmorphism theme applies translucent/frosted-glass surface styles that break readability across multiple UI surfaces — not just the sticky header. Menus (drop-down, context-menu), popovers, and any overlay content also get the glass translucency, making them hard to read as underlying content shows through. This is a broad override problem: the glass theme's surface tokens affect every element that uses --color-surface or --color-surface-raised, including overlays that need opaque backgrounds to function. This likely relates to the extreme theme layering fix (main-full.css layer(bundle-theme)) — now that glassmorphism is properly layered, component styles in higher layers (web-components, bundle-components) should be able to reassert opaque backgrounds on overlays. Fix approach: ensure sticky/fixed elements and overlay surfaces (menus, popovers, dropdowns, dialogs) get opaque or near-opaque backgrounds regardless of theme, either via component-level styles in a higher layer or by scoping the glass effect to exclude these contexts.
close_reason: Added --color-surface-overlay token to surface extension tokens, overrode in glassmorphism theme (light: 95% opaque, dark: 92% opaque), and migrated all sticky/overlay components to use it. Covers sticky headers, footers, bottom nav, table sticky headers, dropdowns, context menus, dialogs, command palette, search, theme picker, time/date/color pickers, tooltips, toasts, shortcuts, combo-box, and settings panel.

title: Scheduled Worker for daily salt rotation and daily_uniques pruning
description:
The visitor-uniqueness scheme relies on a daily rotating salt stored in D1's daily_salts table. New rows land there once per UTC day; daily_uniques grows unbounded with traffic (one row per first-seen visitor per day). Nothing prunes either table right now.

Currently low volume, so no immediate problem, but:
- daily_uniques will grow linearly with unique visitors × days. At scale this becomes the largest table.
- The privacy contract in analytics-spec.md says "truncated at salt rotation" — we're not honouring that.

Scope:
- Add a scheduled Cloudflare Worker (separate from Pages Functions) that runs at UTC midnight.
- DELETE FROM daily_salts WHERE day < DATE('now', '-7 days') — keep a week of history for bot retro analysis
- DELETE FROM daily_uniques — truncate entirely at rotation (yesterday's hashes can't be recomputed anyway, so they'd never match today's)
- Needs wrangler.toml [[triggers]] config or equivalent in the Cloudflare dashboard
- Note: Pages Functions don't support cron triggers directly — this needs to be a separate Worker project or an Analytics Engine scheduled handler

Alternative lightweight fix if we don't want a separate Worker: amortise cleanup into the hit.js path — on ~0.1% of writes, opportunistically DELETE older-than-today rows. Cheaper to ship but ties storage hygiene to request volume.

Reference: admin/r-n-d/analytics/analytics-backend-spec.md "Module Map" and "Daily Hash" sections.

title: Upgrade brand-mark to full web component with dark/light logo switching
description:
## Context

brand-mark is currently a CSS custom element with progressive JS enhancement (80 lines). It lives in src/custom-elements/brand-mark/ but is documented under Design Systems in Web Components. It should be upgraded to a proper web component for richer functionality.

## Current Capabilities
- Text-only display with font styling
- Image injection from `src` attribute
- Wordmark mode (image + text)
- Size variants (s, l, xl)
- Stacked layout (data-stack)
- Tagline support (small/em children)

## Proposed Upgrade

### New Features
1. **Dark/light logo auto-switching** — `src-dark` attribute provides alternate logo for dark mode. Component observes `data-mode` changes and `prefers-color-scheme` to swap automatically.
2. **SVG inline theming** — When src points to an SVG, optionally inline it so `currentColor` and CSS custom properties work for theming.
3. **Responsive logo** — `src-compact` attribute for a smaller/icon-only logo at narrow widths (useful in responsive headers).
4. **Favicon sync** — Optional `favicon` boolean attribute that updates the page favicon to match the current logo.

### Architecture
- Move from src/custom-elements/ to src/web-components/
- Extend VBElement base class
- Keep light DOM (no shadow DOM) so themes can still style text
- Use attributeChangedCallback for reactive src/src-dark switching
- Use matchMedia for prefers-color-scheme observation
- Use MutationObserver on documentElement for data-mode changes

### Migration
- Source code: src/custom-elements/brand-mark/ → src/web-components/brand-mark/
- CSS import: update both index.css and core.css
- JS import: already in core.js, update path
- api.json: update with new attributes
- No markup changes needed for existing users (backwards compatible)
- Current `src` + `alt` + `wordmark` + `height` attributes remain

### Files
- src/web-components/brand-mark/logic.js — new WC implementation
- src/web-components/brand-mark/styles.css — moved from custom-elements
- src/web-components/brand-mark/api.json — new with expanded attributes
- src/custom-elements/brand-mark/ — delete after move
- src/custom-elements/index.css — remove import
- src/custom-elements/core.css — remove import
- src/web-components/index.js — update import path
- src/web-components/core.js — update import path
close_reason: Upgraded brand-mark to full web component using VBElement base class. Added src-dark attribute for automatic dark/light logo switching via prefers-color-scheme + data-mode observation. title: Create <selection-menu> web component — composable text selection toolbar
description:
## Architecture is a generic container that appears as a floating toolbar when text is selected within a target element. It auto-discovers child action components and positions itself above/below the selection. ### Usage Pattern ```html

Selectable content...

``` ### Alternative: Attribute-driven decoupled pattern ```html
...
...
``` This mirrors how popovertarget works — the menu is defined once and multiple elements can reference it by ID via data-selection-menu. This decouples the menu definition from the content, allowing: - One menu shared across many articles - Menu defined in a layout template, content in page body - Dynamic content that gains selection behavior without wrapping ### Research Questions (must answer before implementing) 1. **data-selection-menu vs for= attribute** — Should the menu find its targets (for=), or should targets find their menu (data-selection-menu=)? The data-attribute approach scales better (many targets, one menu). The for= approach is simpler for single-target use. Should we support both? 2. **Relationship to ** — VB already has a `` web component (right-click menus). Selection menus and context menus share: - Floating popover positioning - Triggered by user gesture (selection vs right-click) - Arbitrary child actions - Auto-dismiss behavior Should selection-menu and context-menu share a base class or positioning system? Should there be a generic `` or `` abstraction they both extend? 3. **data-context-menu pattern** — If we add data-selection-menu, should we also formalize data-context-menu as a matching pattern? Currently context-menu wraps its trigger element. A data-context-menu attribute would let any element reference a shared context menu by ID, consistent with the selection-menu pattern. 4. **Event-driven vs direct coupling** — Should the menu communicate with its actions via: - Direct method calls (menu calls action.onSelection(range, text)) - Custom events (menu dispatches 'selection-change' that actions listen to) - Shared context object (menu sets a property that actions read) 5. **Integration with existing highlights-init.js** — The current system is attribute-driven (data-highlights). The new architecture uses web components. Should: - highlight-wc replace highlights-init.js entirely - highlight-wc delegate to highlights-init.js internally - Both coexist (attribute for simple cases, component for composed toolbar) ### Behavior 1. Watches for pointerup on target element(s) 2. When text is selected, positions itself as a floating popover above the selection 3. Passes the selected Range/text to child action components via shared context 4. Auto-hides when selection is collapsed or clicked outside 5. Uses native popover API for the floating panel ### Child Action Components - **highlight-wc**: applies color highlights with a small color palette dropdown (4 circles using --highlight-* tokens, shape follows --radius tokens) - **share-wc** (variant='selection'): shares the selected text quote - **comment-wc**: inline commenting (stub initially — future feature) - **note-wc**: private note attached to a highlight ### Key Design Points - Toolbar is a horizontal row of icon buttons - highlight-wc shows a small color dropdown (4 circles) when clicked - Color circles use --highlight-* tokens, shape follows --radius tokens - Whole toolbar adapts to themes (shadows, borders, radius) - Works standalone OR composed inside selection-menu - data-selection-menu attribute enables declarative wiring without JS ### Files (tentative — pending research answers) - src/web-components/selection-menu/logic.js - src/web-components/selection-menu/styles.css - src/web-components/selection-menu/api.json - src/web-components/note-wc/logic.js (new) - src/web-components/comment-wc/logic.js (new stub) - Modify highlight-wc to work as child of selection-menu - Modify share-wc to support variant='selection' - Investigate shared base with context-menu
close_reason: Implemented full composable selection-menu architecture: <selection-menu> container with positioning/popover, <highlight-wc> with color palette, <note-wc>, <comment-wc> stub. Supports both for= and data-selection-menu= patterns. Old toolbar suppressed when selection-menu manages target.

title: canonical-document spec bump: replace topic with concepts:[] for v1.1 alignment
description:
The canonical-document specification (admin/specs/canonical-document-v1.md) and its implementation (src/lib/canonicalize.js:222) embed `topic: meta(doc, 'vb:topic')` in the canonical JSON used as the input to page hashing and signing. With meta-tag-contract v1.1 (bead ii2k), vb:topic no longer exists in source, so:

- Every page now canonicalizes with topic: null instead of a value, changing every hash from the v1.0 baseline.
- Signed pages from before the migration (commit 293bb7fb) verify against the old hash; new pages are rehashed under the new (incorrect) shape.
- The canonical-document spec is a public verifier contract, so this is a spec revision, not a quick code edit.

Decisions to make in this bead:

1. Field shape:
   a. Replace `topic: string | null` with `concepts: string[]` (sorted alphabetically for stable ordering). Aligns with v1.1.
   b. Remove the field entirely from the canonical JSON (concepts are not signing-relevant — they're navigational metadata, not authorship/integrity).
   Recommendation: (a). Concepts are semantic claims about the document just like author and provenance; preserving them in the hash protects against tampering with topic tags.

2. Spec versioning:
   a. Bump SPEC_VERSION (and the spec filename) to v2 — clean break.
   b. Land as canonical-document-v1.1 — minor bump, since other fields are unchanged.
   Recommendation: (b) — the meta-tag-contract bumped 1.0 → 1.1; canonical-document should track the same minor.

3. Migration story for already-signed pages:
   - The five doc pages signed before commit 293bb7fb have hashes computed against v1.0 canonicalization (with vb:topic dotted-path strings). They need re-signing under v1.1.
   - The signing keys exist (vb-release-demo). The sign-pages.js script runs on every build, so the re-sign happens automatically once canonicalize.js emits v1.1.
   - Question: do we need to record the v1.0 → v1.1 hash change anywhere? (Probably not for pre-release; document the policy in the spec.)

Implementation:
- Edit admin/specs/canonical-document-v1.md (or rename to canonical-document-v1-1.md, decided during the bead).
- Update src/lib/canonicalize.js: replace the topic line with concepts collected from meta[name="concept"] tags, sorted.
- Update SPEC_VERSION in canonicalize.js if needed (per decision 2).
- Update tests/unit/canonicalize.test.js — currently still references vb:topic per S4's report.
- Run npm test; run npm run build; verify dist signature lines match the new hash inputs.

References:
- admin/specs/meta-tag-contract-v1.md (v1.1)
- admin/specs/canonical-document-v1.md (current; needs revision)
- src/lib/canonicalize.js (current; line 222)
- tests/unit/canonicalize.test.js (still references vb:topic per S4 close note)
close_reason:
canonical-document spec bumped to v1.1 to track meta-tag-contract v1.1.

Decisions made:
1. Field shape: replace topic:string with concepts:string[], deduplicated, sorted alphabetically (case-sensitive ASCII via Array.prototype.sort()). Sort is what makes the canonical JSON byte-stable regardless of source-order of <meta name="concept"> tags.
2. Versioning: v1.1 minor (pre-release exception, matching meta-tag-contract). SPEC_VERSION integer stays 1; minor revisions are recorded in spec §K-1 changelog.
3. Migration: scripts/sign-pages.js rehashes on every build, so the 17 doc pages now carry v1.1 hashes (verified — fresh vb:hash + vb:signature on /glossary/). No external v1.0 verifiers to support; pre-release window.

Changes:
- admin/specs/canonical-document-v1.md: heading bumped to v1.1, §G-3 source updated (concept harvesting + sort + dedup), §G serialization rules note that concepts is always an array and MUST be sorted, §K-1 changelog added documenting the pre-release exception.
- src/lib/canonicalize.js: new getConcepts(doc) helper that querySelectorAll's meta[name="concept"], dedups via Set, sorts alphabetically. Replaces topic field in buildCanonicalDocument output.
- tests/unit/canonicalize.test.js: key-order assertion updated to expect 'concepts' instead of 'topic'; primary §G-3 test rewritten to use two concept meta tags in unsorted source order and assert sorted canonical output. Two new tests: dedup-and-sort, and byte-identical-JSON-from-different-source-order.

Test suite: 546/546 pass (2 new tests added). Build: 17 pages signed cleanly under the new canonicalization. analytics-viewcount-v1 contract unaffected.

title: Site-wide sweep: replace bespoke .sr-only with .visually-hidden across components
description:
Following the chart-wc fix in bead zoyk, multiple components still ship their own .sr-only convention. Two are at risk of the same cascade-layer bug that bit chart-wc; the rest are duplicate utilities that should converge on the site-wide src/utils/visually-hidden.css.

Scope (replace 'sr-only' → 'visually-hidden' wherever safe; drop bespoke rules where the site-wide utility already covers them):

JS-emitted classes (light-DOM, switch the className literal):
- src/utils/copy-init.js:91
- src/utils/math-init.js:159
- src/web-components/combo-box/logic.js:100 (live region)
- src/web-components/heading-links/logic.js:144 (announcement)
- src/web-components/page-stats/logic.js:87 (dt)
- src/web-components/page-tour/logic.js:220 (announcer)
- src/web-components/qr-code/logic.js:110 (caption)
- src/web-components/theme-picker/logic.js:169, 207 (span labels)

Static HTML class attributes (light-DOM children, switch the class):
- src/web-components/chat-input/static.html:98
- src/web-components/chat-window/static.html:160
- src/web-components/page-stats/static.html:47, 51
- src/web-components/page-tools/static.html:31
- src/web-components/site-search/static.html:114

Bespoke CSS rules (drop or rename — replaced by site-wide utility for light-DOM cases):
- src/web-components/heading-links/styles.css:62 — uses CSS nesting (& .sr-only). Rename to & .visually-hidden if still needed; otherwise remove.
- src/web-components/page-stats/styles.css:23 — drop the rule; light-DOM dt elements get the utility from main.css.
- src/web-components/qr-code/styles.css:22 — qr-code .sr-only rule. Drop if light-DOM; rename if shadow-internal.
- src/web-components/theme-picker/styles.css:416 — drop or rename per the same rule.

Out of scope (leave alone — namespaced or inside shadow DOM where the global utility doesn't reach):
- src/native-elements/article/highlights.css .hn-sr-only (intentional namespace)
- src/web-components/gantt-chart/styles.css .gc-sr-only (intentional namespace)
- Anything that turns out to be inside a shadow root and needs the rule replicated locally — those keep a scoped .visually-hidden rule rather than reaching for the global one.

Verification:
- Each affected component still hides what it should (visual smoke check).
- Search "sr-only" in src/ returns only namespaced (.hn-, .gc-) and possibly a small set of shadow-DOM-internal cases documented in the close reason.
- Build green; tests green.
- chart-wc remains as zoyk left it (already on .visually-hidden).

Why P2:
- Direct repeat of the chart-wc bug is possible on any component whose cascade layer ordering changes.
- Inconsistency between .sr-only and .visually-hidden across the codebase makes the new accessibility-utilities doc page (bead 5ghb) misleading — it documents .visually-hidden as the convention, but the codebase uses .sr-only widely.

References:
- bead vanilla-breeze-zoyk (the chart-wc fix this sweep follows from)
- bead vanilla-breeze-5ghb (accessibility-utilities doc page that established .visually-hidden as the documented convention)
- src/utils/visually-hidden.css (site-wide utility — single source of truth)
- src/main.css (loads utils into the utils cascade layer)
close_reason:
Site-wide sweep complete. Single source of truth for visually-hidden a11y content is now src/utils/visually-hidden.css (loaded via main.css's utils layer).

Migrated:
- 9 className literals across utils + web components (copy-init, math-init, combo-box, heading-links, page-stats, page-tour, qr-code, theme-picker × 2)
- 5 class attributes in static.html templates (chat-input, chat-window, page-stats × 2, page-tools, site-search)
- 4 bespoke CSS rules dropped (heading-links, page-stats, qr-code, theme-picker)

All four components with bespoke .sr-only rules use light DOM (no shadow DOM), so the site-wide utility reaches their elements directly. Namespaced utilities (.hn-sr-only, .gc-sr-only) are intentional and untouched.

Verification:
- grep '[\"\\'.]sr-only[\"\\' ]' in src/ excluding namespaced returns zero results.
- Tests 546/546 pass.
- CDN bundles rebuilt (component bundles for combo-box, heading-links, page-stats, page-tour, qr-code, theme-picker, plus the master vanilla-breeze.* bundles).
- Site build clean (502 pages).

title: Enhance data-autosave: multi-select, selective retention, auto-derived keys
description:
Polish the existing form draft-persistence helper at src/utils/autosave-init.js based on a comparison with Aaron Gustafson's <form-saver> component (https://www.aaron-gustafson.com/notebook/never-lose-form-progress-again/). VB already covers the core feature via data-autosave on <form>; these are incremental enhancements.

## Tasks

1. **Multi-select correctness fix.** collectData() saves only field.value for <select>, which drops all-but-first selected option for <select multiple>. Iterate selectedOptions and store an array; restore() should set option.selected for each value.

2. **Selective retention via data-autosave-retain.** Add `data-autosave-retain="name email"` (space-separated field names) on <form>. When present, only persist listed fields. Field names not in the list are skipped during collectData() and ignored during restore().

3. **Auto-derived storage key.** When data-autosave is present but empty (boolean form), derive the key from form.method + form.action, falling back to location.pathname + '#' + (form.id || form-index). Document precedence: explicit value wins, derived key otherwise. Update admin/syntax.md.

## Out of scope

- retain-choice consent checkbox UI — separate concern, file as its own bead if needed
- Programmatic API (saveFormState / restoreFormState / clearSavedData) — defer until a caller needs it

## Acceptance

- Existing data-autosave demos still work unchanged
- New Playwright test: <select multiple> round-trip
- New Playwright test: data-autosave-retain limits persistence
- New Playwright test: boolean data-autosave generates a stable key
- admin/syntax.md updated with the two new attributes and boolean form

## Reference

- VB current: src/utils/autosave-init.js
- Source article: https://www.aaron-gustafson.com/notebook/never-lose-form-progress-again/
- Comparison: see chat 2026-05-01
close_reason: Shipped: multi-select round-trip fix, data-autosave-retain allowlist, boolean data-autosave with method+action-derived keys. Includes 8 Playwright behavior tests (desktop+mobile), 4 visual snapshots, basic demo extended with multi-select, new autosave-advanced demo, doc page + admin/syntax.md updated.

title: Deploy /go/* on docs site (eat our own dog food)
description:
Bring the Cloudflare reference implementation (vanilla-breeze-4nav) live on the VB docs site so we use the same /go/* contracts we ask users to implement. Ports the Worker code into Cloudflare Pages Functions (functions/go/*) since the site is already on Pages.

## Scope

1. Port admin/reference-implementations/cloudflare/src/* into functions/go/*
   as Pages Functions. Each route becomes a Functions file
   (e.g. functions/go/notify/messages.js). Reuse the route handlers
   verbatim — they already accept (request, env) signatures compatible
   with Pages Functions. Shared helpers (shared.js) move into
   functions/_lib/go-shared.js next to the existing daily-hash.js.

2. Provision a KV namespace for /go/* storage:
     wrangler kv namespace create VB_GO_KV
   Add the binding to wrangler.toml + the Pages project dashboard.

3. (Optional) configure email transport via Resend:
     wrangler pages secret put RESEND_API_KEY
     wrangler pages secret put RESEND_FROM
   Without these, /go/email runs in record-only mode (audit-trail in KV,
   202 returned, nothing sent). Acceptable for v1 since the docs site
   has no contact form yet.

4. Wire <notification-wc> into the docs-site chrome. Suggested location:
   site/src/includes/header.html, next to the existing settings-panel
   trigger. src points at /go/notify/messages with toast-new disabled
   for the first cut.

5. Seed at least one notification so visitors see the feature working.
   A small admin script (or a manual wrangler kv key put call) writes
   one msg:* entry plus an updated index:msgs entry. Document the
   recipe in admin/reference-implementations/cloudflare/README.md
   under a new 'How VB uses this' section.

6. Update wrangler.toml at repo root to add the VB_GO_KV binding
   (the docs site's wrangler.toml — not the reference impl's).

7. (Stretch) hook /go/feed up to a build-time pipeline that reads
   CHANGELOG entries and writes them into KV via pushFeedEntry. Out of
   scope here; tracked separately in vanilla-breeze-ucs5.

## Verification

- vb.test/go/notify/messages returns the seeded notification (in dev,
  hit it via wrangler pages dev)
- Production https://vanilla-breeze.com/go/notify/messages serves JSON
- The header notification bell shows a badge when there are unread items
- Marking a notification read persists across reload
- /go/__debug/state is NOT exposed in prod (strip it in the port, OR
  guard it behind an env flag)

## Dependencies

- vanilla-breeze-4nav (shipped) — source of the Worker code being ported
- service-contracts docs (shipped, oi44)

## Out of scope

- Express ref impl (separate bead vanilla-breeze-v1fl)
- /go/feed build pipeline (separate bead vanilla-breeze-ucs5)
- Newsletter form (separate bead vanilla-breeze-o2vt)
- Contact form (separate bead vanilla-breeze-m4up)

Once VB_GO_KV is provisioned in the Pages dashboard, /go/* serves live JSON; until then the requireKV() guard returns a 503 envelope instead of 500.

title: Audit nav elements: ul vs menu usage
description:
Audit all `<nav>` elements across the doc site and web components to verify correct use of `<ul>` vs `<menu>`.

## Rule

- `<ul>` inside `<nav>` — for **links** that navigate somewhere (`<a>` elements). This is correct and should remain.
- `<menu>` — for **action controls** (`<button>` elements that do things: delete, edit, toggle). Should NOT be inside `<nav>`.

Both map to `role="list"` in the accessibility tree, but the semantic intent differs. The spec defines `<menu>` as a list of commands/actions, not navigation links.

## Scope

1. Check all `<nav>` elements in layouts, partials, and doc pages — confirm they use `<ul>` (not `<menu>`) when containing `<a>` links.
2. Look for places that currently use `<ul>` but contain only `<button>` controls (toolbars, action groups, component controls) — these are candidates to switch to `<menu>`.
3. Check web component templates (e.g., context-menu, command-palette, settings-panel) for the same pattern.

## Expected Outcome

Small, targeted corrections if any are found. Likely most usage is already correct since `<ul>` is the default. The value is confirming consistency and catching any spots where `<menu>` would be more semantically accurate for button groups.
close_reason: Audit complete — codebase is already correct. One minor mixed-content case in page-layout-landing.html deemed acceptable as-is.

title: Table border conflicts across extreme themes
description:
Table outer borders render incorrectly across multiple extreme themes. The base table styles and theme overrides conflict, producing visible artifacts:

**Observed issues:**
- **Swiss**: Heavy double-border effect on the outer table edge
- **Kawaii**: Square border box visible around rounded corners (border-radius + border-collapse conflict)
- **Brutalist**: Grey border color (likely base table border leaking through instead of theme color)
- **Cyber**: White/light border instead of the expected cyan/purple theme border color

**Root cause (suspected):**
The native `<table>` element styles likely set borders using base tokens (--color-border or hardcoded values) that extreme themes don't fully override. Additionally, `border-collapse: collapse` prevents `border-radius` from working on tables, causing the square-on-round artifact in Kawaii.

**Scope:**
- Audit `src/native-elements/table/styles.css` for border declarations
- Check which border tokens tables use vs what themes override
- Fix the base table border strategy so themes can cleanly control it
- Verify fix across Swiss, Kawaii, Brutalist, Cyber, and all other extreme themes
- Consider whether tables should use `border-collapse: collapse` or `border-spacing: 0` (the latter allows border-radius)
close_reason: Removed table from border-styles auto-apply selector — tables use their own cell border system

title: Analytics: Web Vitals module (LCP, CLS, INP, TTFB)
description:
Ship src/utils/analytics-vitals-init.js (auto-running module). Uses PerformanceObserver to record:
- LCP (Largest Contentful Paint)
- CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift)
- INP (Interaction to Next Paint)
- TTFB (Time to First Byte, from Navigation Timing)

Each metric fires via Analytics.track('perf.lcp', { value: ms, rating: 'good'|'needs-improvement'|'poor' }) etc. following the spec's Tier-3 catalog in admin/r-n-d/analytics/analytics-spec.md.

- LCP reports the last observed entry
- CLS accumulates and reports on page hide
- INP reports the maximum on page hide
- TTFB reports once after load

Thresholds use the web-vitals-compatible cutoffs (LCP ≤2500/4000, CLS ≤0.1/0.25, INP ≤200/500).

Import from src/main-core.js after Analytics.init() so the module can safely call Analytics.track().

No backend changes — events land in the hits table and show up in the /stats/ "Top events" table automatically.

Size budget: <0.8 KB gzip per spec.

Reference: admin/r-n-d/analytics/analytics-spec.md "Web Vitals Module" section.

title: page-toc and page-tools sticky positions collide in docs sidebar
description:
## Symptom

In the docs sidebar (`<aside>` containing `<page-toc>` followed by `<page-tools>`), the two elements use different sticky rules and collide as the user scrolls. The toolbar icons (print, share, text-reader, watch) end up overlapping or wedged between TOC entries — in one observed case the last TOC link renders *below* the icon row.

Reproduced on /docs/elements/web-components/page-info/ (likely any doc with a long TOC on a viewport tall enough to trigger both sticky states).

## What's actually going on

The `<aside>` itself is sticky via the smart-sticky system:

    :root[data-sticky] layout-sidebar > :is(nav, aside)[data-layout-sticky] {
      position: sticky;
      top: calc(var(--sticky-offset, 0px) + var(--sticky-gap, 0px));
      align-self: start;
      max-height: calc(100dvh - var(--sticky-offset, 0px) - var(--sticky-gap, 0px));
      overflow-y: auto;
      overscroll-behavior: contain;
    }

Inside that sticky + scrollable container, **both** children also go sticky:

- `page-toc` → `position: sticky; inset-block-start: calc(var(--sticky-offset) + var(--sticky-gap))` (wide-screen rule)
- `page-tools[data-position="sticky"]` → `position: sticky; inset-block-start: var(--page-tools-offset, var(--size-xl))`

Two different offset tokens and an already-sticky parent. Inner stickiness inside a scrollable sticky container uses the container as the containing block — so as the aside's own overflow scrolls, page-toc pins at its offset while page-tools pins at a *different* offset, and the two bands overlap whenever the TOC is taller than the gap between offsets.

## Suspect fix directions (pick one, don't stack them)

1. **Drop inner stickiness** — the aside is already sticky with its own scroll. The aside IS the scroll container, so inner children don't need to be sticky. Make page-toc and page-tools flow normally inside the sticky aside.
2. **Use a shared offset variable** — if inner stickiness is desired on pages without the smart-sticky aside, unify `--page-tools-offset` and `--sticky-offset` and ensure both children use consistent top values and a stacking gap.
3. **Collapse to a single sticky container** — put page-toc and page-tools inside a shared sticky flex column; only the outer wrapper is sticky.

Option 1 is the smallest change. Option 3 is cleanest structurally.

## Files to touch

- `src/web-components/page-toc/styles.css` (wide-screen sticky rule at `@media (width >= 64rem)`)
- `src/web-components/page-tools/styles.css` (`page-tools[data-position="sticky"]`)
- `src/custom-elements/layout-sidebar/styles.css` (the smart-sticky aside wrapper)
- Four docs layouts (`site/src/layouts/{guide,element,attribute,pattern}.html`) if we change how `<aside>` / `data-layout-sticky` is applied.

## Acceptance

- On a long docs page at viewport ≥ 1024px, scrolling never places page-tools icons overlapping or interleaved with TOC links.
- TOC remains fully visible (all links reachable) while the aside is sticky.
- page-tools stays reachable whether it's pinned above, below, or inside the TOC panel.
- Behavior is consistent across all four docs layouts (guide, element, attribute, pattern).
- Narrow-screen behavior (< 1024px) is unchanged — page-toc collapses to a disclosure at top, page-tools renders inline.

## Repro

1. Open /docs/elements/web-components/page-info/ at ≥ 1280×900.
2. Scroll until page-tools would naturally pin near the top.
3. Observe the icons row sandwiched between TOC links instead of sitting below the full TOC.

(Screenshot captured by user at CleanShot 2026-04-21 at 07.56.58.)

## Out of scope

- Redesigning page-tools variants or orientation logic.
- Changing what belongs in the sidebar (separate discussion).
- Mobile / collapsed-FAB behavior.
close_reason: Collapsed to single sticky container: <html data-sticky>, <aside data-layout-sticky>, removed data-position=sticky from page-tools. Added layout-sidebar rule forcing inner page-toc/page-tools to static inside sticky aside. TOC + tools stay coherent, no collision.

- If conflicts found between specs and shipped components, decide whether

title: Add auto-appearing back-to-top floating link
description:
A back-to-top link that automatically appears when the user scrolls past a threshold and floats in a fixed position. Should be zero-config — just include the element or attribute and it works.

**Behavior:**
- Hidden initially, appears after scrolling past ~1 viewport height
- Smooth scrolls to top on click
- Fades in/out with opacity transition
- Respects prefers-reduced-motion (instant show/hide, no animation)
- Auto-hides near top of page

**Implementation options:**
- Web component: `<back-to-top>` with shadow DOM button
- Data attribute: `data-back-to-top` on body or main that auto-injects the button
- Standalone JS module that self-initializes (like scroll-hide-init.js)

**Position:** Should use the floating button pattern (see related bead). Default bottom-right above any bottom nav.

**Integration:**
- Should coordinate with FAB (button.fab) if both are present — stack or merge
- Should coordinate with sticky bottom nav — position above it
- Uses --safe-bottom for notch-safe positioning

title: Align analytics.js + page-info reads with v1.1 (concepts:[] replaces vb:topic)
description:
Two consumers of the meta-tag contract were not migrated when meta-tag-contract v1.1 replaced vb:topic with <meta name="concept"> (S1, bead ii2k). Both have been silently broken since the migration landed in commit 293bb7fb.

1. src/lib/analytics.js:59-67 — readTaxonomy()

   Harvests meta[name^="vb:"] into the analytics event envelope. With v1.1, vb:topic no longer exists, and the new <meta name="concept"> tags don't match the vb:* prefix selector. Every analytics event since the migration ships without topic/concept data.

   Fix: extend readTaxonomy() to also collect repeated <meta name="concept"> tags into a concepts: string[] field on the envelope. Keep the vb:* harvest for the rest of the namespace (vb:provenance, vb:review, etc. — those are still v1.1).

2. src/web-components/page-info/logic.js:283

   Reads meta('vb:topic') for the disclosure panel. Renders empty topic on every <page-info auto> page.

   Fix: read all repeated <meta name="concept"> tags via querySelectorAll('meta[name="concept"]'), resolve labels via /vocabulary.json when available (or fall back to slug), render as a comma-separated list (or omit if empty). Match the same vocabulary-resolution pattern used by generate-provenance-meta.js for the article-tags footer.

Verification:
- Unit/component test for the new readTaxonomy() shape (events include concepts: array).
- Visual: load /docs/concepts/document-provenance/ and confirm <page-info auto> renders the four concepts (provenance, data-provenance, page-info, meta-tag-contract).
- Run npm run build clean.

Out of scope:
- canonicalize.js — that needs a spec revision; tracked in a separate bead.
- Server-side analytics ingest schema (Cloudflare Pages + D1 + Analytics Engine) — verify the new concepts: field flows through the existing event envelope. If schema changes are needed there, spawn a follow-up bead.

References:
- admin/specs/meta-tag-contract-v1.md (v1.1 contract)
- analytics-subsystem memory entry
- src/web-components/topic-map/logic.js — already SKOS-aware; pattern to mirror
close_reason:
v1.1 alignment for analytics + page-info. Both consumers now read repeated <meta name="concept"> tags instead of the removed vb:topic.

src/lib/analytics.js readTaxonomy(): adds a concepts: string[] field to the event envelope, harvested from all <meta name="concept"> tags. The vb:* harvest is unchanged.

src/web-components/page-info/logic.js #renderFromMeta(): replaced const topic = meta('vb:topic') with a queryAll on meta[name="concept"]. The Topic section of the disclosure panel now renders concepts as a comma-separated list of links to /topics/{id}/, with rel="tag" and data-concept for analytics. Slugs are used as labels (kebab-case readable; full prefLabels remain in the build-time article-tags footer that the layouts inject after page-info).

Tests: full suite (544 tests) green. No analytics or page-info tests touched the topic field directly. canonicalize.test.js still references vb:topic — that's owned by bead 5a1y (canonical-document spec bump).

title: Analytics: one-shot cleanup of legacy is_unique rows
description:
Before the c92309f6 fix, checkUnique() ran for every event type, not just page.view. Some rows in the production hits table have is_unique=1 on non-page.view events (ui.extension_toggle, theme-composer composition events, etc.). The stats totals over-count unique visitors by whatever number of these mis-flagged rows exist.

Run once against the remote D1:

  npx wrangler d1 execute vanilla-breeze-analytics --remote \
    --command "UPDATE hits SET is_unique = 0 WHERE event_name <> 'page.view';"

Trivial but ensures /stats/ totals are trustworthy historically. Safe to run — no rows added or removed, just flag cleanup. Idempotent.

After running, close this issue.
close_reason:
Verified against production D1: 0 rows match `is_unique = 1 AND event_name <> 'page.view'`. No cleanup required — the daily_uniques schema migration (0002) dropped the legacy hash data, and subsequent hits after c92309f6 have used the correct logic. The only is_unique=1 row in the hits table is on a page.view event, which is the intended state.

SQL verified:
  SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hits WHERE is_unique = 1 AND event_name <> 'page.view';
  -> affected_rows: 0

title: Build <watch-wc> web component (encapsulate page-watch)
description:
Wrap the page-watch button + state into a drop-in web component that doesn't require authoring a button + icon + aria-label by hand. Today's pattern is:

  <button type="button" data-watch-page class="ghost icon-only" aria-label="Watch this page for updates">
    <icon-wc name="eye" size="sm"></icon-wc>
  </button>

That's noisy enough that we put it in four layouts. The component lets users (and us) write:

  <watch-wc></watch-wc>

…and get the same behavior plus consistent styling, accessibility, and the same VBStore('watches') / page-watch:add|remove events.

## Scope

- New src/web-components/watch-wc/ with logic.js, styles.css, api.json, static.html, and a register entry in src/web-components/core.js + src/web-components/index.js + core.css import
- Component renders <button data-watch-page> internally so the existing src/utils/page-watch-init.js continues to handle the watch state machine — the WC is a thin author-friendly wrapper, not a re-implementation
- Attributes:
  - variant="icon" (default) | "compact" | "button" — three visual presets matching how page-tools, settings-panel, and standalone use look
  - label="Watch this page" — overrides the default label text for the button variant
  - server-sync (boolean) — sets window.vbPageWatch.serverSync at attribute-read time (per-instance opt-in instead of meta tag)
- Reactive: aria-pressed reflects watched state; component listens for page-watch:add/remove and re-syncs (so two <watch-wc> instances on the same page stay in sync)
- Switch the four docs layouts to use <watch-wc> and remove the verbose button markup
- Doc page at /docs/elements/web-components/watch-wc/ + cross-link from /docs/attributes/data-watch-page/
- Demo at /docs/examples/demos/watch-wc-basic.html with the three variants

## Verification

- npm test green (no unit tests are required but existing suite should still pass)
- Site builds; docs layouts render the new component instead of the bare button
- Watch toggle still persists state, fires page-watch:add/remove, surfaces in notification-wc Watching tab
- Two <watch-wc> instances on a page stay synced (toggle one, the other reflects)

## Out of scope

- Re-implementing the FNV-1a hash / VBStore writes — those stay in src/utils/page-watch-init.js; the component is a wrapper.
- Server-side cron polling for watched pages — separate future work.
close_reason: Shipped src/web-components/watch-wc/ with three variants (icon/compact/button), sibling sync via page-watch events, server-sync attribute, htmlvalidate schema. Replaces verbose button in all four docs layouts. Doc page + demo + cross-link from data-watch-page Related.

title: Add <ol> list markup to breadcrumb navs for a11y
description:
## Context All 44 breadcrumb `
` elements across the site use bare ``/`` without list markup. VB's CSS already supports `nav.breadcrumb > ol` (provides `display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: center; gap` plus separator styling via `li:not(:last-child)::after`). Adding `
    ` is correct for breadcrumbs since they represent an ordered path. ## Scope 44 instances across two categories: ### Category 1: Site doc pages (~37 instances) Breadcrumbs in Astro pages following this pattern: ```html
    Snippets / Tabs
    ``` Should become: ```html
    1. Snippets
    2. Tabs
    ``` The `/` separator text should be removed — VB's `nav.breadcrumb li:not(:last-child)::after` generates separators via CSS (`content: "/"` by default, configurable via `data-separator`). Files (site/pages/docs/): - snippets/**/*.astro (~12 files) - elements/**/*.astro (~5 files) - examples/extensions/*.astro (~6 files) - integrations/*.astro (2 files) - lab/experiments/*.astro (~4 files) - lab/theme-picker/*.astro (2 files) ### Category 2: Demo HTML pages (~7 instances) Breadcrumbs in example demo pages, mostly single back links like: ```html
    ← Back to Examples
    ``` These single-link breadcrumbs could either: - Get `
      /
    1. ` wrapping for consistency - Or stay as-is since they're just back links (not real breadcrumb trails) Files (docs/examples/demos/): - dashboard-analytics.html, grid-identity.html, layout-attributes.html, layout-attributes-refactored.html, layout-comparison.html, media-basic.html, regions-basic.html ## CSS Already Supports This From `src/native-elements/nav/styles.css`: - `nav ul, nav ol { list-style: none; margin: 0; padding: 0; }` — list reset - `nav.breadcrumb > ol { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: center; gap }` — flex layout - `nav.breadcrumb li:not(:last-child)::after { content: "/" }` — auto separators - Separator variants: `data-separator="chevron|arrow|dot|pipe"` No CSS changes needed. ## Approach 1. Write a script to find all breadcrumb navs and transform them 2. For multi-segment breadcrumbs: wrap in `
        /
      1. `, remove literal `/` separators 3. For single back-link breadcrumbs: add `
          /
        1. ` for consistency 4. Verify `nav.breadcrumb` class is present on all breadcrumb navs (some may be missing it) 5. Build and visually verify separators render correctly
close_reason: All 47 breadcrumb navs across 46 files wrapped in <ol>/<li>. CSS auto-generates separators. Verified build + visual check.

title: text-reader stops when reading <code> tags containing markup
description:
## Symptom text-reader's playback halts (silent, or stuck on one paragraph) when the speech synthesis run hits an inline `` element whose textContent contains HTML-looking markup — e.g. `<page-toc levels="h2,h3">` or the heavily-escaped snippets inside ``. Observed on pages that dogfood the toolbar, notably: - /docs/concepts/go-convention/ (many `/go/...` and `<...>` code tokens) - /docs/concepts/service-facade/ (Cloudflare Worker sample, escaped HTML) - /docs/elements/web-components/text-reader/ (meta — the reader's own docs page, full of escaped tag examples) ## Suspicion Likely one of: 1. **Text extraction re-interprets markup.** `src/web-components/text-reader/logic.js#buildParagraphMap` walks `p,li` via `selectors` and pulls `textContent`. `textContent` should already return the decoded text (so `<page-toc>` becomes literal ``). If that literal is later fed to `innerHTML`-based highlight scaffolding or string templated into a wrapper, the `<` would be mis-parsed and swallow the rest of the paragraph — speech stops mid-word or silently skips ahead. 2. **Highlight range math goes off by one.** The CSS Custom Highlight API tracks word ranges across the extracted text. If highlight construction includes an intermediate `innerHTML` step, angle brackets in code content may shift or truncate the range, causing the utterance boundary events to never fire and `#keepAlive` to sit on a stale pointer. 3. **Web Speech API chokes on specific characters.** Some engines refuse utterances containing backticks, NULs, or unpaired angle brackets; the utterance errors silently and `onend` never fires. Check `onerror` on the utterance and confirm it's wired. ## Investigation hints - `src/web-components/text-reader/logic.js` — look at: - `#buildParagraphMap` (extracts text from `selectors` default `p,li`) - how `#fullText` is assembled from paragraphs - how the highlight Range is built (anything touching `innerHTML` is suspect) - whether `this.#utterance.onerror` logs or silently drops errors - Repro: navigate to /docs/concepts/service-facade/, press play on the toolbar text-reader. Note which paragraph it stops on and inspect that paragraph's DOM. - Compare against a code-free page (e.g. an index page) to confirm the bug is scoped to markup-heavy content. ## Acceptance - text-reader reads through a page containing `` with escaped markup (like /docs/concepts/go-convention/) end-to-end without stalling. - Errors thrown by `SpeechSynthesisUtterance` are surfaced to the console so this class of bug doesn't hide again. - Add one regression test or sample page combining `

`, `

  • `, and `` with angle-bracket content and verify the extracted `#fullText` matches the expected literal string. ## Out of scope - Changing the default `selectors` list or the `for`-target contract. - Adding SSML support. - Highlight styling tweaks.
close_reason: Browser TTS engines treat literal < as SSML start — text extracted from inline <code> (e.g. 'Future <geo-map>') caused silent onend halt. Fix: sanitize angle brackets to spaces in #speak before passing to SpeechSynthesisUtterance; same-length substitution preserves charIndex → highlight alignment. Also added console.warn on utterance.onerror to surface future silent-halt regressions.

title: Analytics dashboard enhancements: time-series, session flow, props breakdown
description:
Current /stats/ is totals + top-10 tables. Useful but flat. Enhancements (in rough order of leverage):

1. Time-series chart — hits per hour (24h window) or per day (7d/30d). SVG or vb-charts. One query: SELECT date_trunc-equivalent, COUNT(*) GROUP BY bucket.

2. Event props breakdown — pick an event (e.g. table.sort) and see the distribution of its props (column=A count, column=B count, etc.). Requires the `props` TEXT column (JSON) to be queryable; D1 supports json_extract, so SELECT json_extract(props, '$.column'), COUNT(*) FROM hits WHERE event_name = ?1 GROUP BY 1.

3. Session flow — recent events grouped by approximate session (e.g. requests within 30min of each other from the same country/event-pattern). Harder because we don't store session IDs; could be heuristic.

4. Per-event filter in the UI — dropdown that scopes the whole dashboard to one event_name.

5. Referrer quality — split internal vs external, highlight top external sources.

Scope: pick 1 and 2 for a first pass. 3 and 4 are bigger.

Reference: admin/r-n-d/analytics/analytics-spec.md "Dashboard Query Reference" section in the backend spec has template queries.
close_reason:
Delivered in commit d3621427:
- Time-series chart (hourly/daily bucket) via <chart-wc data-type="line">
- Top events bar chart via <chart-wc data-type="bar">
- All tabular panels migrated to <data-table> with sortable columns

title: Harden content-swap structural CSS so author display rules can't break stacking
description: display:grid on the swap container and grid-area:1/1 on faces are structural invariants — without them faces don't overlap and the swap appears broken. They live in the web-components layer, so any unlayered author display:flex/block beats them. Lock these structural rules with !important (only the structural minimum, not theming hooks) so authors can style freely without defeating the component's layout contract.
close_reason: Locked display:grid on the swap container and grid-area:1/1 on faces with !important. These are structural invariants — the swap layout breaks without them. Author CSS (themes, demos, page styles) still controls everything else (transitions, transforms, padding, borders, backgrounds). Reverted the previous demo workaround; class='face-content' with display:flex on the container now works because the !important display:grid wins.

title: Fix Kitchen Sink page: stray Nunjucks syntax rendering as visible text
description:
The Kitchen Sink documentation page (/docs/examples/kitchen-sink/) has unconverted Nunjucks template syntax rendering as visible text on the page.

**Symptoms:**
- `{# ================================================================ 1. Design Tokens ================================================================ #}` showing as raw text
- `{# --- layout-stack --- #}` and similar Nunjucks comments visible throughout
- Likely other Nunjucks artifacts ({%%, {{, {#) that should have been stripped during the 11ty-to-Cook migration

**Root cause:**
The site was migrated from 11ty (Nunjucks templates) to Cook SSG. Nunjucks comment syntax `{# ... #}` is not recognized by Cook and passes through as literal text instead of being stripped.

**Fix approach:**
- Convert the Kitchen Sink page from .njk syntax to plain HTML
- Remove all `{# ... #}` comment blocks (replace with HTML comments `<!-- ... -->` if needed)
- Check for any `{% ... %}` or `{{ ... }}` template expressions that also need conversion
- Verify the page renders correctly after cleanup

**File:** `site/src/pages/docs/examples/kitchen-sink.html` (confirm exact path)

title: Refactor highlights toolbar to Medium-style action icons with color palette
description:
## Context

The current highlights toolbar shows color swatches as the primary UI. Medium's approach is better: action-first with 4 icon buttons (highlight, share, respond, note), and colors as a secondary dropdown from the highlight button.

## Current State
- Toolbar shows: [yellow][green][blue][pink] color swatches + optional 'Add Note' / 'Remove' buttons
- Colors are the primary interaction, actions are secondary
- No share or respond functionality
- Toolbar appears above text selection

## Proposed Redesign

### New Toolbar Layout
`[highlight-icon ▾] [share-icon] [respond-icon] [note-icon]`

4 icon buttons in a horizontal row:
1. **Highlight** (highlighter icon) — default action, applies the last-used color. Click the dropdown chevron to open a color palette.
2. **Share** — shares the selected text quote (Web Share API or copy link)
3. **Respond** — future: inline comment/response (stub for now)
4. **Note** — adds a private note to the selection/highlight

### Color Palette (dropdown from highlight button)
- 4 small circles in a row (matches theme)
- Uses existing --highlight-yellow/green/blue/pink tokens
- Circle or square shape based on theme --radius tokens
- Active color gets a checkmark
- Opens below the highlight icon button

### Files to Modify
- src/utils/highlights-init.js — refactor showToolbar() to render icon buttons + color dropdown
- src/native-elements/article/highlights.css — restyle toolbar, add icon styles, dropdown panel
- Lucide icons needed: highlighter, share-2, message-circle, sticky-note

title: Provision + deploy /go/* on production Cloudflare Pages
description:
The functions/go/* Pages Functions and functions/_lib/go/* handlers ship in code (vanilla-breeze-8tje, vanilla-breeze-4nav, vanilla-breeze-ucs5, vanilla-breeze-m4up, vanilla-breeze-o2vt). The header bell (notification-wc) and the watch button on every docs page are wired up. But none of this actually works in production until the KV namespace is provisioned and bound, and (for /go/email) a transport is configured. This bead is the manual cutover.

## Steps

1. Provision the KV namespace
     wrangler kv namespace create VB_GO_KV
   Copy the returned id into wrangler.toml (replace REPLACE_WITH_VB_GO_KV_ID), AND bind it in the Cloudflare dashboard (Pages project → Settings → Functions → KV namespace bindings).

2. (Optional but recommended) configure Resend for /go/email
     wrangler pages secret put RESEND_API_KEY
     wrangler pages secret put RESEND_FROM
   Without this /go/email runs in record-only mode (audit trail in KV under email:{id}, 202 returned, nothing actually sent).

3. Seed at least one notification so the bell badge is non-empty for new visitors:
     wrangler kv key put --binding=VB_GO_KV "msg:welcome" \
       '{"id":"welcome","type":"update","title":"Welcome","body":"Vanilla Breeze docs use the same /go/notify endpoint we ask you to implement.","date":"2026-04-21T00:00:00Z","read":false,"dismissed":false,"priority":"normal"}'
     wrangler kv key put --binding=VB_GO_KV "index:msgs" '["welcome"]'

4. (Optional) seed page-watch sample subscriptions if we want to demo the server-sync path. Most users won't have it on initially — server-sync is opt-in via meta tag.

5. Deploy via the existing CI (push to main → CF Pages auto-deploy) or manually:
     wrangler pages deploy site/dist/pages

6. Smoke test in production:
   - GET https://vanilla-breeze.com/go/notify/messages → returns the seeded msg JSON, NOT a 503
   - The header bell shows '1' badge on a fresh browser (clear localStorage first)
   - Click bell → 'Welcome' notification renders inside the panel
   - Mark read → reload → badge gone
   - GET https://vanilla-breeze.com/go/feed → returns CHANGELOG entries from the bundled feed-data.js fallback (or KV if seeded)
   - GET https://vanilla-breeze.com/go/__debug/state → STRIP THIS before deploying. It currently exists only in the dev-stub server.mjs, NOT in functions/go/, so production is already safe; verify.
   - Visit any docs page (e.g. /docs/concepts/go-convention/) → click the eye-icon watch button → switch to Watching tab in the bell → entry appears

7. Watch the Pages Function metrics in the CF dashboard for the first 24 hours; tune NEWSLETTER_LISTS env var if needed.

## Verification

- All endpoints in the smoke list above respond with the expected shape
- The 503 'service_unavailable' envelope from the requireKV() guard is NOT seen on production after step 1
- The 'Welcome' notification disappears from the bell after the user marks it read (state persists in KV across requests, not just localStorage)
- /go/email returns 202 even without RESEND_*; with secrets, a real email arrives

## Dependencies

- vanilla-breeze-4nav (Cloudflare reference impl) — shipped
- vanilla-breeze-8tje (Pages Functions deploy of /go/*) — shipped
- vanilla-breeze-ucs5 (CHANGELOG → /go/feed pipeline) — shipped
- vanilla-breeze-m4up (contact form via /go/email) — shipped
- vanilla-breeze-o2vt (/go/newsletter signup form) — shipped

## Out of scope

- D1 / Postgres migration of /go/* state (KV is enough for v1)
- Multi-user auth on /go/notify (single-tenant for v1)
- Cron worker for re-checking page-watch subscriptions (separate future work)

title: Investigate sticky section nav for long doc pages
description:
Long documentation pages like /docs/elements/web-components/page-toc/ have sidebar navigation that scrolls away with the page. The sidebar should optionally stick so users can always jump between sections.

**The problem:**
On element doc pages, the left sidebar nav disappears as you scroll. Users have to scroll back to the top to navigate to a different section. This is especially painful on pages with many sections (attributes, examples, API tables).

**Proposed approach:**
A data-attribute (behavior, not style) like `data-sticky-nav` or `data-scroll-lock` on the sidebar `<nav>` or `<aside>` that makes it position: sticky within its parent. The sidebar scrolls its own overflow if its content is taller than the viewport.

**Key considerations:**
- Should be `data-*` attribute based (behavior) not a CSS class (style)
- Must handle sidebar content taller than viewport (internal overflow-y: auto)
- Should respect the site header height (sticky top offset = header height)
- Needs to deactivate on mobile where sidebars stack vertically
- Consider interaction with data-page-layout sidebar/split layouts
- Check if data-layout-sticky on the existing layout system already handles this or if it needs extension

**Reference:**
- Current sidebar in doc layouts: `site/src/layouts/docs.html` and `site/src/layouts/element.html`
- Existing sticky header: `header[data-sticky]` and `header[data-scroll-hide]`
- Layout attributes: `src/custom-elements/layout-attributes.css` (data-layout-sticky exists but may not handle sidebar case)
close_reason:
Implemented three features from this bead and vanilla-breeze-pktb:
1. Sticky sidebar nav via data-layout-sticky on layout-sidebar children (CSS) + overflow-x:clip on body/main in doc layouts to unblock position:sticky
2. Horizontal page-toc variant via orientation=horizontal attribute (CSS only)
3. Back-to-top CSS utility with scroll-driven animation (data-back-to-top)
Applied data-layout-sticky to all 4 doc layouts (element, attribute, pattern, guide).

description: Six small additions inspired by https://jakub.kr/writing/details-that-make-interfaces-feel-better. Most of the article's ideas (text-wrap balance/pretty, tabular-nums on numeric surfaces, font-smoothing antialiased, staggered entry, separate enter/exit motion durations, reduced-motion respect) are already shipped in VB. This bead fills the remaining gaps and adds a docs landing that pulls everything together. ## Deliverables 1. `--radius-inner` concentric helper in `src/tokens/radius.css` — `calc(var(--_radius) - var(--_inset, 0px))` so nested elements automatically get the right radius. 2. `img[data-outlined]` rule — opt-in 1px subtle outline + outline-offset: -1px for image definition without layout impact. New file `src/native-elements/image/styles.css` if absent. 3. `--shadow-flush` (or appropriately-named) token in `src/tokens/shadows.css` — multi-layer box-shadow as an alternative to flat borders for elevated surfaces. 4. `vb-blur-in` / `vb-blur-out` keyframes in `src/tokens/extensions/motion-fx.css` + `[data-animate="blur-in"]` selector hook. opacity + scale + filter:blur. 5. ` { font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums }` default in `src/native-elements/time/styles.css`. Currently shipped on a few specific surfaces; making it the element default catches every page-info date, every changelog timestamp, etc. 6. New docs page at `/docs/concepts/feel/` (or polish/micro-interactions) naming the seven techniques (six above + the "transitions for interruptible state, keyframes for one-shot" guideline), what VB does today, and crediting the article. Wired into Concepts grid card list, nav, and the effect-compositing Related section. ## Acceptance - All five code changes ship and load - `data-animate="blur-in"` runs the new keyframe; collapses under prefers-reduced-motion - `` elements on /docs/concepts/go-convention/ have tabular-nums in computed style - /docs/concepts/feel/ renders with breadcrumb above h1 (per rbm2 layout slot pattern) - Concepts index card grid includes the new entry - conformance has no new warnings on touched files ## Out of scope - Optical alignment (design discipline, not a primitive) - JS-driven concentric radius helper (pure CSS via calc()) - Wholesale rewrite of base styles (outline is opt-in) - Replacing all flat borders with shadow borders - Motion library port (VB stays CSS-first) Plan reference: /Users/tpowell/.claude/plans/review-admin-r-n-d-april13-plan-notifica-reactive-matsumoto.md
close_reason: Shipped four code additions (--radius-inner concentric helper, img[data-outlined], --shadow-flush, vb-blur-in/out + data-animate hook) plus /docs/concepts/feel/ landing that names what VB already ships globally and credits the source article. title: Move brand-mark to design systems section, consider web component upgrade
description:
## Context

brand-mark is currently listed under Custom Elements in the sidebar navigation and is a CSS-only inert custom element with optional JS for image injection. It probably belongs in a Design Systems section since it's a foundational branding element, not a generic layout primitive.

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Navigation Reorganization
Move brand-mark from the Custom Elements list to a Design Systems or Branding section in the sidebar. Consider grouping it with related site-level elements like site-tools, site-legal, and mobile-menu under a 'Site Structure' or 'Design Systems' category.

### 2. Web Component Upgrade (Evaluate)
Consider upgrading brand-mark from a CSS custom element to a full web component for richer functionality:
- Shadow DOM encapsulation for style isolation across themes
- Responsive logo switching (different logos for different breakpoints or color modes)
- Automatic light/dark logo swap via prefers-color-scheme or data-mode
- SVG inline injection for theming (fill/stroke via currentColor)
- Animated transitions between logo states
- Favicon synchronization

### Current State
- CSS: src/custom-elements/brand-mark/styles.css
- JS: src/custom-elements/brand-mark/logic.js (progressive enhancement for img injection)
- Doc: site/src/pages/docs/elements/custom-elements/brand-mark.html
- Nav: site/data/navigation.json in elements.custom.items

### Files to Modify
- site/data/navigation.json — move to new section
