title: page-tools icons duplicate after print preview is cancelled (text-reader, watch-wc)
description:
## Symptom

After clicking the print icon in the docs `<page-tools>` toolbar and then cancelling the browser's print dialog, `<text-reader>` and `<watch-wc>` each render **two** icons. `<print-page>` and `<share-wc>` stay single.

Observed layout after repro: print, share, volume, volume, eye → wrap → eye.

Screenshot: CleanShot 2026-04-21 at 07.59.31.

## Repro

1. Open any docs page with the toolbar (e.g. /docs/elements/web-components/page-info/).
2. Click the print icon in page-tools — the browser print preview opens.
3. Cancel the print dialog.
4. Observe that the reader (volume) and watch (eye) icons are doubled.

## Suspect cause

text-reader and watch-wc both `appendChild` a newly-created `<button>` inside their `setup()` without first clearing existing DOM. If `setup()` runs a second time for any reason, a second button is appended and the old one is left in place. print-page appears to clear (`this.innerHTML = ''`) before rendering, which is why it survives the same trigger.

Likely triggers for a second `setup()`:

- `@media print { page-tools { display: none !important; } }` changes page-tools' box during the preview. On cancel, components inside may go through a reconnect cycle in some engines, firing `connectedCallback` → `setup()` again.
- Stimulus/Turbo or the docs' smart-sticky MutationObserver may re-run component lifecycle after a layout event from the print dialog.
- VBElement base class may not guard `setup()` against being invoked twice.

## Investigation

- `src/web-components/text-reader/logic.js` — check `setup()` / `#renderIcon()`; verify whether it guards against pre-existing `[part="trigger"]` children.
- `src/web-components/watch-wc/logic.js` — check `setup()`; it currently does `this.#button = this.#renderButton(variant); this.appendChild(this.#button);` with no clear and no existence check.
- `src/web-components/print-page/logic.js` — note the `this.innerHTML = '';` pattern; this is the shape to match.
- `src/lib/vb-element.js` — confirm whether `setup()` is idempotent by contract, or whether subclasses must be defensive.

Add a console log at the top of each setup and repro to confirm which component(s) re-enter.

## Acceptance

- After cancelling (or completing) a print preview, the toolbar has exactly one of each icon.
- Repeated print-cancel cycles never accumulate duplicates.
- Fix applied uniformly: setup() in text-reader and watch-wc becomes idempotent — either it clears prior rendered children or short-circuits when its own output already exists.
- If the root cause turns out to be a framework-level `connectedCallback` re-entry, document the idempotence contract in the VBElement base-class JSDoc.

## Out of scope

- Other print-related regressions (dialog styling, afterprint cleanup).
- Audit of every other web component for the same pattern — follow-up bead if the shared fix surfaces more cases.
close_reason: Fixed idempotence in text-reader and watch-wc: teardown() now removes rendered buttons; setup() strips any pre-existing trigger before appending. Verified via forced disconnect/reconnect — neither component accumulates duplicates.

title: Explore CustomStateSet adoption: convention for :state() vs data-*
description:
Investigate where CustomStateSet (ElementInternals.states + CSS :state()) should be used in Vanilla Breeze, and document the convention vs data-* attributes.

## Context
CustomStateSet (https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CustomStateSet) lets custom elements expose internal state to CSS via :state(name) without mutating DOM attributes. VB currently uses data-* for both author-declared config AND component-computed state, which blurs the line.

## Proposed split (to validate)
- **data-*** — author-facing inputs declared in HTML source (data-autosave, data-theme, data-layout="reel")
- **:state()** — component-computed runtime outputs (is-animating, loading, has-overflow, hydrated, busy)

Rule of thumb: data-* is the input from the author, :state() is the output from the component.

## Deliverables
1. Audit existing components for data-* attributes that are actually computed by JS (not author-set) — these are migration candidates
   - Likely candidates: nav-bar active tracking signals, reel-wc overflow flags, any 'hydrated' / 'ready' markers
2. Document the convention in admin/syntax.md or a new admin/specs/ doc
3. Consider browser support (Safari 16.4+, all modern evergreen — fine for VB)
4. Decide on naming convention for state names (kebab-case vs camelCase; prefix convention?)
5. Identify any components that should ship :state() in v1 vs migrate later
6. Note tradeoffs: :state() is invisible in DevTools element panel, can't querySelector by attribute, but cleaner API surface

## Open questions
- Should we keep aria-current et al. as-is (semantic, accessible) and only use :state() for non-semantic runtime flags?
- Reflect both (data-* AND :state()) for inspectability during migration?
- Any conflict with existing CSS that uses [data-*] selectors heavily?

## Out of scope
Actual migration of components — this bead is just the convention + audit. Migration work spawns child beads.

title: Investigate page-toc extensions: horizontal variant, back-to-top, sticky forms
description:
Explore extending the page-toc web component with three new capabilities:

**1. Horizontal variant**
Current page-toc is vertical sidebar-style. Investigate a horizontal variant (e.g. sticky top bar or inline breadcrumb-style) for mobile and single-page layouts where vertical sidebar space isn't available.

**2. Back-to-top links**
Auto-inject a 'Back to top' link at the end of each major section, or provide a floating back-to-top button that appears after scrolling past the first section. Should integrate with page-toc's existing IntersectionObserver scroll-spy.

**3. Sticky form navigation (horizontal + vertical)**
For long multi-section forms (checkout, onboarding, settings), a sticky section indicator that shows current position and allows jumping between form sections. Should work in both horizontal (top bar) and vertical (sidebar) orientations. Could complement or integrate with the wizard system.

**Questions to answer:**
- Should these be page-toc variants (attributes) or separate components?
- How does horizontal page-toc interact with auto-hiding headers?
- Should back-to-top be part of page-toc or a standalone utility?
- Can sticky form nav reuse page-toc's scroll-spy internals?
close_reason:
Investigation complete. Recommendations:

**1. Horizontal variant → page-toc attribute**
Add `orientation="horizontal"` attribute. The scroll-spy internals (IntersectionObserver, heading tracking, active link management) are fully decoupled from layout — only CSS needs to change (flex-direction: row, horizontal padding instead of indentation, border-block-end instead of border-inline-start). Keep in same component since 90% of logic is shared. On mobile, horizontal variant would replace the collapsible details with a scrollable horizontal bar.

**2. Back-to-top → standalone CSS utility**
Back-to-top is NOT a page-toc concern — it's a page-level affordance. Implement as a `data-back-to-top` attribute on an `<a href="#">` element with CSS scroll-driven animation to show/hide. Pure CSS: `animation-timeline: scroll()` with opacity keyframes. No JS needed. If we want auto-injection, that's a separate tiny component (`<back-to-top>`).

**3. Sticky form nav → page-toc with form sections**
page-toc already works with any heading levels — forms with `<fieldset><legend>` sections can be scoped. Add a `sections` attribute as alternative to `levels` that accepts a CSS selector (e.g. `sections="fieldset"`) to scan for non-heading landmarks. The scroll-spy and active-state logic is identical. Horizontal orientation (from #1) makes this natural for form wizards.

**Architecture decision:** All three are variants/extensions of page-toc, not separate components. The scroll-spy internals are already cleanly separated from the view layer.

title: Retire normalize-legacy-nunjucks plugin: migrate 6 source spots to clean Cook patterns
description:
The site/plugins/normalize-legacy-nunjucks.js plugin exists only to translate three legacy 11ty/Nunjucks idioms still scattered through the source tree. Once those spots are rewritten in native Cook patterns, the plugin can go and we have one less mechanical translator in the build chain.

Spots to rewrite:

1. {{ '...' | escape }} inside <code-block> (transforms to <code-block data-cook-esc="...">)
   - site/src/pages/docs/fluid-scaling.html:114
   - site/src/pages/docs/patterns/forms/wizard.html (lines ~636, 651, 663, 676)

2. {{ themeRegistry | dump | safe }} inside inline <script> (transforms to JSON.stringify literal)
   - site/src/pages/docs/tools/theme-lab/index.html:434, 441

3. {{ '{' }} / {{ '}' }} literal-brace helpers — none currently used, but the regex is in the plugin (defensive). Confirm no live uses.

Rewrite strategy:

- For pattern 1: pre-encode the string content as base64 of escaped HTML (matches what the plugin produces) and emit the <code-block data-cook-esc="..."> directly. OR simpler: just emit <code-block data-escape>literal HTML</code-block> if Cook's data-escape attribute handles the escaping at render time without needing data-cook-esc. Check site/node_modules/cook-ssg behavior on data-escape first.

- For pattern 2: theme-lab is rendered by render-dynamic-pages.js anyway — that plugin already substitutes content from BORDER_STYLES / VIGNETTES. Move the JSON.stringify into render-dynamic-pages.js so the page never carries dump/safe syntax in source.

After all spots are migrated:

- Delete site/plugins/normalize-legacy-nunjucks.js
- Remove 'normalize-legacy-nunjucks' from site/config/main.js plugins.default[]
- Run npm run build; verify the affected pages render identically (the three doc pages above must still show their code samples and theme-lab must still render its previews).

Out of scope:

- Other Nunjucks-looking syntax in source (e.g. {% extends %} inside <code-block data-escape>) is literal example code, not interpreted, and should stay.
- render-dynamic-pages.js itself is unrelated and stays.

title: Breadcrumbs inconsistent across Guide/Concepts pages — missing, misplaced, or duplicated
description:
## Symptoms Breadcrumb rendering across `layout: guide` pages (notably the Concepts group and the broader Guide tab) is inconsistent in three ways: 1. **Misplaced** — pages like `/docs/concepts/go-convention/` and `/docs/concepts/service-contracts/` render the breadcrumb BELOW the `

` and lead paragraph instead of above them. Root cause: `guide.html` layout renders `${title}` + `${description}` before `${content}`, so any `
` the page author inlines at the top of their content ends up under the heading. 2. **Missing** — `/docs/concepts/effect-compositing/` has no breadcrumb at all. Same layout, no inline crumb added. Most other `layout: guide` pages outside Concepts (typography, mobile, accessibility, semantic-layouts, etc.) also have no breadcrumb. 3. **Inconsistent depth** — two variants exist in Concepts pages that DO have breadcrumbs: - 3-level: `Docs > Concepts > ` (architecture-decisions, resilience-matrix) - 2-level: `Concepts > <title>` (form-association, go-convention, service-contracts, service-facade) ## Structural root cause `site/src/layouts/guide.html` contains no breadcrumb — each page is expected to hand-roll one in its content block. `attribute.html` / `element.html` / `pattern.html` layouts DO handle breadcrumbs in the template above the `<h1>`. The guide layout is the outlier. ## Fix direction Add a breadcrumb to `site/src/layouts/guide.html` between the `<article>` opening tag and the `<h1>`. Use a named slot (`${slot:breadcrumb}`) so each page supplies its own `<template slot="breadcrumb">` block (cook-ssg has no conditionals or truthy checks, so a slot is cleaner than `${var}` fallbacks that would print literal placeholders if unset). Then for every page using `layout: guide`: - Remove any inline `<nav class="breadcrumb">` that currently sits inside content - Add `<template slot="breadcrumb"><nav class="breadcrumb" aria-label="Breadcrumb"><ol>…</ol></nav></template>` at the top of the body - Standardize depth to `Docs > <Group name> > <title>` (3 levels). Group landing URLs: - Concepts → /docs/concepts/ - Layouts → /docs/semantic-layouts/ - Styling → /docs/typography/ - Accessibility → /docs/accessibility/ - Platform → /docs/mobile/ ## Scope - Phase 1 (this bead): Fix the 8 Concepts pages (7 detail pages + index) so placement, presence, and depth are all correct. Update guide.html layout to render the breadcrumb slot. - Phase 2 (follow-up if this lands cleanly): add breadcrumbs to the remaining `layout: guide` pages (Layouts / Styling / Accessibility / Platform groups, ~15 pages). - Out of scope: Start-tab pages use `layout: docs` and render their own h1 inside content — separate bead if we want breadcrumbs there. ## Acceptance - `/docs/concepts/effect-compositing/` renders a breadcrumb. - Every Concepts page renders: `Docs > Concepts > <title>` above the `<h1>`. - No page renders a breadcrumb below the `<h1>`. - `/docs/concepts/go-convention/` and `/docs/concepts/service-contracts/` specifically show the fix (these were the user-reported offenders). - `npm run build` passes and the validator has no new warnings on the touched files. ## Repro - Open `/docs/concepts/go-convention/` — breadcrumb renders below "/go/ Convention" heading (wrong). - Open `/docs/concepts/effect-compositing/` — no breadcrumb visible. - Open `/docs/concepts/architecture-decisions/` — breadcrumb is present and correct depth (acts as the reference).

title: Explore <input type=emoji> progressive enhancement to emoji-picker
description:
Investigate a progressive enhancement pattern where `<input type="emoji">` (an unrecognized input type that browsers render as a text field) gets upgraded to VB's `<emoji-picker>` web component.

## Concept

Browsers treat unknown input types as `type="text"`, so `<input type="emoji" name="reaction">` works natively as a plain text field. JavaScript detects the custom type and replaces/wraps it with the full emoji-picker experience.

## Questions to Explore

- **Form integration**: How does the upgraded element submit its value? Hidden input, ElementInternals, or form-associated custom element?
- **Fallback UX**: The plain text input should accept emoji pasted or typed via OS picker. Is that sufficient as a no-JS baseline?
- **Detection pattern**: `document.querySelectorAll('input[type="emoji"]')` vs MutationObserver vs explicit `data-enhance="emoji"` opt-in?
- **Attribute forwarding**: Which input attributes (`name`, `required`, `value`, `placeholder`, `disabled`) carry over to the picker?
- **Multiple selection**: Single emoji (reaction) vs multi-emoji (message composition) — does the input pattern support both?
- **Prior art**: Check how other frameworks handle custom input type upgrades (e.g., Shoelace, Lion Web Components).

## Aligns With

- Existing `emoji-picker` web component
- VB progressive enhancement philosophy (HTML-first, JS-third)
- The `data-*` attribute enhancer pattern already used for `data-switch`, `data-range`, etc.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix usability and contrast issues in glassmorphism theme
description:
The glassmorphism theme has usability and contrast problems in both light and dark modes that likely fail WCAG AA contrast requirements.

**Known issues to evaluate:**

Light mode:
- Translucent surfaces (0.55 opacity white) over gradient mesh background may produce insufficient text contrast depending on which gradient color is behind the text
- Glass borders (0.2 opacity) are nearly invisible, making interactive boundaries unclear
- Form inputs with translucent backgrounds (0.2 opacity white) have low contrast for placeholder text
- Code blocks with 0.4 opacity backgrounds may not provide enough contrast for syntax-highlighted code

Dark mode:
- Surface opacity drops to 0.08 — text on near-transparent surfaces over dark gradient may lose contrast
- Border opacity at 0.1 makes card/section boundaries invisible
- Input fields at 0.06 opacity are essentially invisible — users can't see where to type
- Shadow opacity values may not create visible elevation distinction on dark backgrounds

**What to check:**
1. Run contrast checks on all text/background combinations in both modes
2. Verify form controls are visually identifiable (input boundaries, focus states)
3. Check that interactive elements (buttons, links, cards) have clear boundaries
4. Verify the gradient mesh background doesn't create contrast dead zones where text becomes unreadable
5. Test with the new opacity token system (--glass-surface-opacity etc.) to see if tuning values fixes issues without breaking the aesthetic

**Files:**
- `src/tokens/themes/_extreme-glassmorphism.css` (theme definition, recently refactored to use opacity tokens)
- `src/utils/surface-types.css` (surface type implementations)
- `src/tokens/extensions/surfaces.css` (opacity token scale + semantic glass tokens)

**Context:** The theme was recently refactored to use opacity tokens (--opacity-5 through --opacity-90 and semantic --glass-*-opacity tokens). This makes it possible to tune all transparency values from one place rather than hunting through 77 hardcoded values. The fix should adjust token values, not reintroduce hardcoded opacities.
close_reason: Bumped dark mode glass opacity tokens for better contrast: surfaces 8%→12%, borders 10%→15%, inputs/code blocks get explicit stronger values. Light mode unchanged.

title: calendar-wc: replace internal day-detail overlay with composed day-view component
description:
calendar-wc's #openDayDetail (logic.js lines 746-896) builds its own hour grid from <div> elements — duplicating what day-view now provides semantically. Replace the internal overlay with an embedded <day-view>.

PREREQUISITE GAPS in day-view that must be addressed first:
1. Auto hour range — day-view needs a mode where it scans its calendar-event children and auto-computes start/end hour (first event hour - 1 to last event hour + 1), matching the overlay's sparse behavior. Could be data-start-hour='auto' or a new data-mode='auto-range'.
2. Compact presentation — the overlay fits in a positioned dialog, not a full-page view. Day-view needs to work well at small sizes without the full working-hours grid.
3. Event data translation — calendar-wc passes events as JS objects with {label, time, color, icon}. Day-view expects authored HTML. The integration needs JS to generate <calendar-event> elements from the event data.

Once these gaps are filled, the overlay becomes: create a <day-view data-start-hour='auto'>, populate with <calendar-event> elements from the event data, and embed in the existing dialog container.
close_reason: Replaced calendar-wc day-detail overlay with composed day-view component. Added auto hour range mode and compact variant to day-view. Removed duplicated hour-grid code from calendar-wc.

title: Add opt-in grid identity system for page layouts
description:
Extract grid identity concept from zen-garden as opt-in modules. Semantic HTML elements auto-register grid areas based on document structure.

## Reference
See r-n-d/grid-identity-option.md for full implementation details including complete CSS code.

## Concept
Instead of manually assigning grid areas, semantic elements get them automatically:
- body > header → grid-area: body-header
- body > nav → grid-area: body-nav
- main > article → grid-area: main-article

2. src/custom-elements/layout-page.css (page templates)
3. Demo page showing page-stack, page-sidebar, page-holy-grail layouts

## Key Features
- Opt-in (not in main bundle by default)
- Works with pure semantic HTML
- Includes :has() adaptations (adjust when nav absent)
- Responsive breakpoints built in
- Complementary to existing layout-attributes system

- [ ] page-sidebar layout demo functional
- [ ] page-holy-grail layout demo functional

title: Build-time auto-link of vocabulary terms in article prose (dfn for prefLabel, abbr for altLabel)
description: admin/r-n-d/evaluate/glossary-system.md flagged optional build-time auto-linking as an extension point. The shape, now that the vocabulary + element-doc semantics are aligned, is: For each article HTML during the Cook build: - Scan the prose (excluding code/pre/headings/anchors and excluding [data-no-glossary] regions if added). - For the FIRST occurrence of any concept's prefLabel, wrap with: match - For the FIRST occurrence of any altLabel, wrap with: match - Subsequent occurrences in the same article: plain (no dfn, no abbr). - Match prefLabels case-insensitively at word boundaries; altLabel matching depends on length (treat single-letter altLabels with caution to avoid false positives). - Skip if the author already authored a manual mention of that concept (any existing a[data-concept="{id}"] in the article). Prerequisites: - vocabulary.json (shipped — site/src/_data/vocabulary.json). - definitions.json + popover (shipped — see bead 05bu). - Authoring docs already cover the manual pattern; auto-link is the optional progressive layer on top. Why P3: - Manual authoring pattern works today for the small doc corpus (5–17 pages with concepts). - Auto-link adds maintenance value once the corpus grows (10x+ pages) or once contributors who don't know the pattern start writing. - Risks: false matches on common words (e.g. 'review' is an altLabel for data-review); aggressive linking turns prose into noise. The build needs a tunable allowlist or per-concept opt-in flag. References: - admin/r-n-d/evaluate/glossary-system.md (Extension Points section) - site/plugins/generate-glossary.js (where this work would slot in or pair with) - site/plugins/generate-provenance-meta.js (already loads vocabulary; could share the cache) - site/src/pages/docs/concepts/glossary-terms.html (manual pattern doc) - site/src/pages/docs/elements/native/dfn.html, abbr.html (semantic anchors for the dfn vs abbr choice) Out of scope here: - Full natural-language matching (stemming, plurals, possessives) — start lexical/case-folded only. - Live editor preview of auto-linked content — build-time only.

title: a-rel: link-relation upscaling system (CSS hooks + safe outbound + rel index)
description:
Implement the link-relation upscaling proposal in `admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/a-rel-plan.md`. Treat the native HTML `rel` attribute on `<a>` as a first-class semantic vocabulary VB can style and enhance, so authors prefer `rel` for link meaning and reserve `class` for visual variants and `data-*` for app config.

## Why

`rel` is standardised, machine-readable, ASCII-tokenised, and works natively with attribute selectors (`a[rel~="external"]`). Sites today underuse it and reach for ad-hoc classes / data-attrs for things HTML already names. Picking up `rel` everywhere gives VB:

- safer outbound links by default (no opener leak)
- semantic CSS styling for external / help / tag / next / prev / legal links with zero classes
- a JS-discoverable index of links by intent that other VB features can build on (legal-nav discovery, sequence pagination, contextual help, etc.)

## Deliverables

Three small artefacts, each shippable independently:

1. **`src/utils/links.css`** — semantic stylesheet for the high-value tokens. Style external (`↗` glyph), help (dotted underline), tag (pill), prev/next (arrows), legal (`license` / `privacy-policy` / `terms-of-service`) muted. CSS-only — works with no JS.
2. **`src/utils/links.js`** — passive enhancer:
   - `upgradeBlankTargets(root)` — for every `a[href][target="_blank"]` without `opener`/`noopener`/`noreferrer`, add `noopener`. Idempotent.
   - `collectLinksByRel(root)` → `Map<token, HTMLAnchorElement[]>` so other utilities can query.
   - Optional opt-in privacy mode that adds `noreferrer` (data attribute on `<html>` or programmatic flag).
3. **Docs page** at `/docs/concepts/link-relations/` — vocabulary table (security, document, navigation, classification), VB philosophy (rel for semantics; class for variants; data-* for app config), examples for CSS + JS upgrade, list of supported tokens, and explicit "anti-patterns" (no `rel="big-button"`).

## Spec source

`admin/r-n-d/linking-and-navigation/a-rel-plan.md` — 614 lines, includes the "high-value rel tokens" matrix, the 5-rung authoring ladder, and a complete reference impl of `upgradeBlankTargets()` and `collectLinksByRel()`.

## Acceptance

- Every `<a target="_blank">` on the docs site that didn't already specify a rel-opener policy ends up with `rel="… noopener"` after JS upgrade. Verify on /docs/concepts/go-convention/ (has external links).
- `a[rel~="external"]::after` arrow renders on external doc links without any class added by the author.
- The 4-token footer pattern (`license` / `privacy-policy` / `terms-of-service` / `external`) renders consistently across themes.
- New docs page at /docs/concepts/link-relations/ uses the breadcrumb slot pattern (per vanilla-breeze-rbm2) and is linked from the Concepts index card grid.
- htmlvalidate config is unchanged (rel is a native attribute).

## Out of scope

- Higher-level "rel components" (no `<rel-router>`, no `<rel-paginator>`). The plan explicitly stops at native enhancement; component layer is a future bead if demand emerges.
- Inline help expansion for `rel="help"` (Experiment 5 in the spec) — interesting but separate bead.
- Audit script that flags missing rel on outbound links — separate bead if we want CI enforcement.

## Closing

When the three artefacts ship and the docs page renders cleanly:

    bd close vanilla-breeze-<id> --reason="Shipped utils/links.{css,js} + /docs/concepts/link-relations/. Outbound _blank links now safe-by-default; semantic CSS for external/help/tag/prev/next/legal works with zero author classes."

title: Add floating button position system (data-float)
description:
A general-purpose positioning system for floating action buttons, back-to-top links, page-info triggers, chat widgets, and similar fixed-position UI elements.

**The pattern:**
A `data-float` attribute (or similar) that positions an element in one of 8 standard screen positions:
- `top-left`, `top-right`
- `center-left`, `center-right`
- `bottom-left`, `bottom-center`, `bottom-right`
- `top-center`

**CSS implementation:**
```css
[data-float] {
  position: fixed;
  z-index: var(--z-float, 200);
  margin: var(--size-m);
}
[data-float='bottom-right'] {
  inset-block-end: calc(var(--size-m) + var(--safe-bottom, 0px));
  inset-inline-end: var(--size-m);
}
/* ... other positions ... */
```

**Key considerations:**
- Must account for `--safe-bottom` / `--safe-top` on edge positions
- Must stack properly when multiple floats occupy the same corner (auto gap)
- Should shift up when bottom nav is present (detect nav.bottom or add offset token)
- Needs mobile-aware sizing (larger touch targets on coarse pointer)
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion for any entry/exit animations
- Consider `data-float-offset` for custom offset from edge

**Use cases:**
- button.fab (currently has its own fixed positioning — migrate to this)
- Back-to-top link
- page-info toggle button
- Chat/support widget trigger
- Cookie consent dismiss button
- Floating share button
- Debug/dev tools toggle

**Relationship to existing code:**
- `button.fab` in `src/utils/corporate-cta.css` currently hardcodes position: fixed with specific insets — should be refactored to use this system
- `[data-sticky-cta]` was already removed (consolidated into footer[data-sticky]) so no conflict there

title: Validator tests: install deps via npm ci instead of npx to fix CI races
description:
17 test files in tests/unit/validators/*.test.js shell out via `execSync` to `npx pa11y`, `npx cspell`, `npx html-validate`, etc. When node --test runs them in parallel on CI, the concurrent npx downloads race in the shared npm cache and throw:

  npm error ENOTEMPTY: directory not empty, rmdir '.../node_modules/@cspell/dict-software-terms/dict'

Plus when a download partially fails, subsequent `npx pa11y` calls find incomplete modules and throw `Cannot find package 'chalk'...` etc.

Symptom: quality-gate CI has been failing for months with 5-15 validator test failures per run. Locally everything passes because npx has time to resolve sequentially.

Proper fix: declare pa11y, cspell, html-validate, htmlhint, and the other validators as devDependencies in package.json and invoke them via `./node_modules/.bin/<tool>` (or just `npx --no <tool>` so npx errors if missing). `npm ci` in CI then installs them deterministically, no race.

Pa11y specifically also needs puppeteer's Chromium; either install it via the puppeteer postinstall (if listed as dep) or use Playwright's chromium via PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH.

Interim: pa11y tests already skip in CI (e7974d79). Cspell and the rest still fail. Broader --skip-in-CI coverage would hide the signal without fixing the root.

Out-of-scope but related:
- The integration.test.js test file imports files from ../fixtures and shells out per-validator; consolidating into a single deterministic suite would be cleaner.
- quality-gate job currently uses `continue-on-error` via the commented `needs: quality-gate` on `build`. Restoring the gate after this lands would be the real win.

title: chart-wc: replace bespoke .sr-only with site-wide .visually-hidden utility
description:
On https://vanilla-breeze.com/stats/, the page scrolls ~2150px past the visible footer (html.scrollHeight=5952 vs visible content ending ~3800px). Cause: the data-table sibling that <chart-wc> renders for screen readers is class="sr-only" and its CSS rule (chart-wc > table.sr-only in vanilla-breeze-charts.css) is partially overridden by cascade layers: position/clip/overflow apply but width/height do NOT, so the table renders at ~270 × 5236 instead of 1 × 1. Position:absolute keeps it visually clipped (no visible bleed) but scrollHeight reaches its bottom edge → ~2150px of phantom empty scroll past the footer.

Same shape as the popover_display_gotcha memory: a utility rule competing with cascade-layered selectors loses on subset of properties.

The bespoke chart-wc > table.sr-only utility is also broken outside chart-wc ancestors (any future use of class='sr-only' on the site gets zero hiding treatment), and duplicates the responsibility of src/utils/visually-hidden.css which is already the site-wide convention.

Scope:
1. Find where <chart-wc> constructs its sr-only data-table (likely src/web-components/chart-wc/logic.js or similar).
2. Change the generated <table>'s class from sr-only to visually-hidden so it picks up the site-wide utility.
3. Remove the chart-wc > table.sr-only rule from src/web-components/chart-wc/styles.css (or wherever the rule lives that compiles into vanilla-breeze-charts.css).
4. Verify visually-hidden.css is in the chart bundle's loaded chain — if it's not, decide between (a) explicitly importing it from chart-wc's styles or (b) relying on it being globally loaded on every page that uses chart-wc.
5. Rebuild CDN bundle (npm run build:cdn or whatever the chart bundle build is) so the production stats page picks it up.

Verification:
- Browser load /stats/: html.scrollHeight should match (or only modestly exceed) html.offsetHeight; no phantom scroll past footer.
- The screen-reader data table is still in the DOM and accessible (class="visually-hidden", clipped to 1×1, accessible to assistive tech).
- All other pages using chart-wc still hide their data table.

References:
- https://vanilla-breeze.com/stats/ (live repro)
- src/web-components/chart-wc/{logic.js,styles.css}
- src/utils/visually-hidden.css (site-wide utility)
- /docs/accessibility-utilities/ (newly added doc page documenting the visually-hidden utility — bead 5ghb)
- popover_display_gotcha memory (related cascade-layer pattern)
close_reason:
Shipped. chart-wc/logic.js now applies class='visually-hidden' to the source data table (was 'sr-only'); the bespoke chart-wc > table.sr-only rule is removed from styles.css. Site-wide .visually-hidden utility (src/utils/visually-hidden.css, loaded via main.css's utils layer) handles the actual hiding. Print override preserved as chart-wc > table.visually-hidden so the data table un-hides when printing.

CDN bundles rebuilt (vanilla-breeze-charts.{css,js} + components/chart-wc.js); production stats page picks up the fix on next deploy. Tests 546/546 green; site build clean.

Verified locally: vanilla-breeze-charts.css contains zero 'table.sr-only' references after rebuild.

Other components (page-stats, theme-picker) still roll their own .sr-only rules — out of scope for this bead but could be tackled in a follow-up sweep.
